Identification.LongTitle: An Act respecting the taxation of spirits, wine and tobacco and the treatment of ships’ stores

Provision.Text: This enactment introduces a modern framework for the taxation of spirits, wine and tobacco. It re-enacts existing provisions in the Excise Act and the Excise Tax Act relating to the excise levies on these products, together with technical improvements, and incorporates a range of new provisions. The key features of the enactment include the following:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the continued imposition of a production levy on spirits, tobacco products and raw leaf tobacco and the replacement of the existing excise levy on sales of wine with a production levy at an equivalent rate;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the replacement of the excise duty and excise tax on tobacco products other than cigars with a single excise duty;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: the introduction of excise warehouses to allow for the deferral of the payment of the production levy on domestic and imported spirits and wine to the time of sale to the retailer;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: more comprehensive licensing requirements and new registration requirements for persons carrying on activities in relation to goods subject to duty;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: explicit recognition of limited exemptions for certain goods produced by individuals for their personal use;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: tight new controls on the possession and distribution of goods on which duty has not been paid;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: modern provisions concerning the use of spirits and wine for non-beverage purposes and the use of specially denatured alcohol;

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: updated administrative provisions, including new remittance, assessment and appeal provisions that are similar to those under the Goods and Services Tax/Harmonized Sales Tax legislation;

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: updated enforcement provisions, including new offence, penalty and collection provisions; and

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: transitional provisions applicable to spirits, wine and tobacco products produced before the enactment comes into force.

This enactment also implements changes to the ships’ stores provisions, which were announced by the government on September 27, 2001. These changes broaden the enabling legislation for ships’ stores regulations and implement a temporary fuel tax rebate program for certain ships that, as a result of amendments to the Ships’ Stores Regulations effective June 1, 2002, will no longer qualify for ships’ stores relief.
Furthermore, this enactment implements the tobacco tax increases announced by the government on November 1, 2001.

Finally, the replacement of the existing provisions in the Excise Act and the Excise Tax Act relating to the excise levies on spirits, wine and tobacco necessitates consequential amendments to those Acts as well as other Acts, including the Budget Implementation Act, 2000, the Canada Customs and Revenue Agency Act, the Criminal Code, the Customs Act, the Customs and Excise Offshore Application Act, the Customs Tariff, the Export Act, the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act, the Special Economic Measures Act and the Tax Court of Canada Act.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Excise Act, 2001.
The definitions in this section apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: absolute ethyl alcohol means the substance with the chemical composition C2H5OH.

accredited representative means a person who is entitled under the Foreign Missions and International Organizations Act to the tax exemptions specified in Article 34 of the Convention set out in Schedule I to that Act or in Article 49 of the Convention set out in Schedule II to that Act.
Agency means the Canada Customs and Revenue Agency established under subsection 4(1) of the Canada Customs and Revenue Agency Act.
alcohol means spirits or wine.
alcohol licensee means a person who is a spirits licensee or a wine licensee.
alcohol registrant means a person who holds an alcohol registration issued under section 17.
analyst means a person who is designated as an analyst under section 11.
approved formulation means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any product made with alcohol by a licensed user in accordance with a formula for which the user has approval from the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any imported product that, in the opinion of the Minister, would be a product under paragraph (a) if it were made in Canada by a licensed user.

Definition.Text: assessment means an assessment under this Act and includes a reassessment.
beer means beer or malt liquor as defined in section 4 of the Excise Act.

black stock, in respect of manufactured tobacco, means that the tobacco is stamped but not marked in accordance with any statute of a province to indicate that it is intended for retail sale in a particular province or in particular provinces.

bottle-your-own premises means premises in which, in accordance with the laws of the province in which they are located, alcohol is supplied from a marked special container of alcohol for the purpose of being packaged by a purchaser.
bulk, in respect of alcohol, means alcohol that is not packaged.
cigar includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a cigarillo or cheroot; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any roll or tubular construction intended for smoking that consists of a filler composed of pieces of natural or reconstituted leaf tobacco, a binder of natural or reconstituted leaf tobacco in which the filler is wrapped and a wrapper of natural or reconstituted leaf tobacco.

Definition.Text: cigarette includes any roll or tubular construction intended for smoking, other than a cigar or a tobacco stick. If a cigarette exceeds 102 mm in length, each portion of 76 mm or less is considered to be a separate cigarette.
Commissioner means the Commissioner of Customs and Revenue, appointed under section 25 of the Canada Customs and Revenue Agency Act.
container, in respect of a tobacco product, means a wrapper, package, carton, box, crate or other container that contains the tobacco product.
customs bonded carrier means a person who transports or causes to be transported goods in accordance with section 20 of the Customs Act.
customs bonded warehouse means a place that is licensed as a bonded warehouse under the Customs Tariff.
customs bonded warehouse licensee means a person licensed under the Customs Tariff to operate a bonded warehouse.
data means representations, in any form, of information or concepts.
denature means to denature spirits into denatured alcohol or specially denatured alcohol using prescribed denaturants in the prescribed manner.
denatured alcohol means any prescribed grade of denatured alcohol made from spirits in accordance with the prescribed specification for that grade.

duty means, unless a contrary intention appears, the duty imposed under this Act and the duty levied under section 21.1 or 21.2 of the Customs Tariff and, except in Parts 3 and 4, includes special duty.
duty free shop means a place that is licensed as a duty free shop under the Customs Act.

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: exploitant agréé de boutique hors taxes

Definition.Text: duty free shop licensee means a person licensed under the Customs Act to operate a duty free shop.
duty-paid market means the market for goods in respect of which duty, other than special duty, is payable.
duty-paid value means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of imported cigars, the value of the cigars as it would be determined for the purpose of calculating an ad valorem duty on the cigars in accordance with the Customs Act, whether or not the cigars are subject to ad valorem duty, plus the amount of any duty imposed on the cigars under section 42 of this Act and section 20 of the Customs Tariff; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of imported cigars that, when imported, are contained in containers or otherwise prepared for sale, the total of the value of the cigars as determined in accordance with paragraph (a) and the value similarly determined of the container in which they are contained.

Definition.Text: excise warehouse means the premises of an excise warehouse licensee that are specified by the Minister as the excise warehouse of the licensee.
excise warehouse licensee means a person who holds an excise warehouse licence issued under section 19.
export means to export from Canada.
ferment-on- premises facility means the premises of a ferment-on-premises registrant that are specified by the Minister as the registrant’s ferment-on-premises facility.
ferment-on- premises registrant means a person who holds a ferment-on-premises registration issued under section 15.
fiscal month means a fiscal month as determined under section 159.

foreign duty free shop means a retail store that is located in a country other than Canada and that is authorized under the laws of that country to sell goods free of certain duties and taxes to individuals who are about to leave that country.

foreign ships’ stores means tobacco products taken on board a vessel or aircraft while the vessel or aircraft is outside Canada and that are intended for consumption by or sale to the passengers or crew while the passengers and crew are on board the vessel or aircraft.
Her Majesty means Her Majesty in right of Canada.
import means to import into Canada.
intoxicating liquor has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act.
judge, in respect of any matter, means a judge of a superior court having jurisdiction in the province in which the matter arises or a judge of the Federal Court.
licensed tobacco dealer means a person who holds a tobacco dealer’s licence issued under section 14.
licensed user means a person who holds a user’s licence issued under section 14.
liquor authority means a government board, commission or agency that is authorized by the laws of a province to sell intoxicating liquor.

manufacture, in respect of a tobacco product, includes any step in the preparation or working up of raw leaf tobacco into the tobacco product. It includes packing, stemming, reconstituting, converting or packaging the raw leaf tobacco or tobacco product.
manufactured tobacco means every article, other than a cigar or packaged raw leaf tobacco, that is manufactured in whole or in part from raw leaf tobacco by any process.
mark means, in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a special container of spirits, to mark in the prescribed form and manner to indicate that the container is intended for

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: delivery to and use by a registered user, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: delivery to and use at a bottle-your-own premises; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a special container of wine, to mark in the prescribed form and manner to indicate that the container is intended for delivery to and use at a bottle-your-own premises.

Definition.Text: Minister means the Minister of National Revenue.
month means a period beginning on a particular day in a calendar month and ending on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day immediately before the day in the next calendar month that has the same calendar number as the particular day; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the next calendar month does not have a day that has the same calendar number as the particular day, the last day of that next calendar month.

Definition.Text: non-duty-paid, in respect of packaged alcohol, means that duty, other than special duty, has not been paid on the alcohol.

officer, except in sections 167, 226 and 296, means a person who is appointed or employed in the administration or enforcement of this Act, a member of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police or a member of a police force designated under subsection 10(1).
packaged means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of raw leaf tobacco or a tobacco product, packaged in a prescribed package; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of alcohol, packaged

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in a container of a capacity of not more than 100 L that is ordinarily sold to consumers without the alcohol being repackaged, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in a marked special container.

Definition.Text: partially manufactured tobacco means manufactured tobacco that is cut filler or cut rag or that is manufactured less fully than cut filler or cut rag.
peace officer has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Criminal Code.

person means an individual, a partnership, a corporation, a trust, the estate of a deceased individual, a government or a body that is a society, a union, a club, an association, a commission or another organization of any kind.

personal use, in relation to the use of a good by an individual, means the use of the good by the individual or by others at the individual’s expense. It does not include the sale or other commercial use of the good.
prescribed means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a form or the manner of filing a form, authorized by the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of the information to be given on or with a form, specified by the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, prescribed by regulation or determined in accordance with rules prescribed by regulation.

Definition.Text: produce means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of spirits, to bring into existence by distillation or other process or to recover; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of wine, to bring into existence by fermentation.

Definition.Text: raw leaf tobacco means unmanufactured tobacco or the leaves and stems of the tobacco plant.
record means any material on which data are recorded or marked and which is capable of being read or understood by a person or a computer system or other device.
registered user means a person who holds a user’s registration issued under section 16.
responsible, in relation to a person, means that the person is responsible for bulk alcohol in accordance with sections 104 to 121.
sale price, in respect of cigars, means the total of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount charged as the price for the cigars before an amount payable in respect of a tax under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act is added,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount charged as the price for or in respect of the container in which the cigars are contained,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that the purchaser is liable to pay to the vendor by reason of or in respect of the sale of the cigars in addition to the amount charged as the price, whether payable at the same or any other time, including, but not limited to, any amount charged for or to make provision for advertising, financing, commissions or any other matter, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of duty imposed on the cigars under section 42.

Definition.Text: SDA registrant means a person who holds a specially denatured alcohol registration issued under section 18.
special container means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of spirits, a container of a capacity of more than 100 L but not more than 1,500 L; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of wine, a container of a capacity of more than 100 L.

Definition.Text: special duty means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a tobacco product, a special duty imposed under subsection 53(1), 54(2) or 56(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of imported spirits, the special duty imposed under subsection 133(1).

Definition.Text: special excise warehouse means the premises of a special excise warehouse licensee that are specified by the Minister as the special excise warehouse of the licensee.
special excise warehouse licensee means a person who holds a special excise warehouse licence issued under section 20.
specially denatured alcohol means any prescribed grade of specially denatured alcohol made from spirits in accordance with the prescribed specification for that grade.
specified premises, in respect of a licensed user, means the premises of the licensed user that are specified by the Minister under subsection 23(3).
spirits means any material or substance containing more than 0.5% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume nother than

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: wine;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: beer;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: vinegar;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: denatured alcohol;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: specially denatured alcohol;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an approved formulation; or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any product containing or manufactured from a material or substance referred to in paragraphs (b) to (f) that is not consumable as a beverage.

Definition.Text: spirits licensee means a person who holds a spirits licence issued under section 14.

stamped, in respect of a tobacco product, means that all prescribed information in a prescribed format is, in the prescribed manner, stamped, impressed, printed or marked on, indented into or affixed to the product or its container to indicate that duty, other than special duty, has been paid on the product.
sufferance warehouse has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act.
sufferance warehouse licensee means a person licensed under the Customs Act to operate a sufferance warehouse.
take for use, in respect of alcohol, means to consume, analyze or destroy alcohol or to use alcohol for any purpose that results in a product other than alcohol.
Tax Court means the Tax Court of Canada.
tobacco dealer means a person, other than a tobacco licensee, who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: purchases for resale, sells or offers to sell raw leaf tobacco on which duty is not imposed under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: does not take physical possession of the tobacco.

Definition.Text: tobacco licensee means a person who holds a tobacco licence issued under section 14.
tobacco marking means prescribed information that is required under this Act to be printed on or affixed to a container of tobacco products that are not required under this Act to be stamped.
tobacco product means manufactured tobacco, packaged raw leaf tobacco or cigars.

tobacco stick means any roll or tubular construction of tobacco intended for smoking, other than a cigar, that requires further preparation to be consumed. If a tobacco stick exceeds 90 mm in length or 800 mg, each portion of 60 mm or less or each portion of 650 mg or less, respectively, is considered to be a separate stick.
wine means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a beverage, containing more than 0.5% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume, that is produced without distillation, other than distillation to reduce the absolute ethyl alcohol content, by the alcoholic fermentation of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an agricultural product other than grain,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a plant or plant product, other than grain, that is not an agricultural product, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a product wholly or partially derived from an agricultural product or plant or plant product other than grain;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sake; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a beverage described by paragraph (a) or (b) that is fortified not in excess of 22.9% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume.

Definition.Text: wine licensee means a person who holds a wine licence issued under section 14.

Section.Text: A reference in this Act to a repealed enactment, or a portion of it, of the legislature of a province or territory shall, with respect to a subsequent transaction, matter or thing, be read as a reference to the provisions of any enactment replacing the repealed enactment or portion that relate to the same subject-matter as the repealed enactment or portion. If there is no replacement enactment or portion, or if there are no provisions in the replacement enactment that relate to the same subject-matter, the repealed enactment or portion shall be read as unrepealed in so far as is necessary to maintain or give effect to the reference.

Section.MarginalNote: Meaning of administration or enforcement of this Act
Section.Text: For greater certainty, a reference in this Act to administration or enforcement of this Act includes the collection of any amount payable under this Act.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsections 30(1) and 32(1), section 61, subsections 70(1) and 88(1) and sections 230 and 231, if one of two or more persons, with the knowledge and consent of the rest of them, has anything in the person’s possession, it is deemed to be in the custody and possession of each and all of them.
In this section and in subsections 30(1) and 32(1), section 61 and subsections 70(1) and 88(1), possession means not only having in one’s own personal possession but also knowingly

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: having in the actual possession or custody of another person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: having in any place, whether belonging to or occupied by one’s self or not, for one’s own use or benefit or that of another person.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: related persons are deemed not to deal with each other at arm’s length; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is a question of fact whether persons not related to each other were, at any particular time, dealing with each other at arm’s length.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, persons are related to each other if they are related persons within the meaning of subsections 251(2) to (6) of the Income Tax Act, except that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a reference in those subsections to “corporation” shall be read as a reference to “corporation or partnership”; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a reference in those subsections to “shares” or “shareholders” shall, in respect of a partnership, be read as a reference to “rights” or “partners”, respectively.

Section.Text: This Act is binding on Her Majesty and Her Majesty in right of a province.
The Minister shall administer and enforce this Act and the Commissioner may exercise the powers and perform the duties of the Minister under this Act.

Subsection.Text: The officers, employees and agents that are necessary to administer and enforce this Act shall be appointed, employed or engaged in the manner authorized by law.

The Minister may authorize a designated officer or agent or a class of officers or agents to exercise powers or perform duties of the Minister, including any judicial or quasi-judicial power or duty of the Minister, under this Act.

The Minister and the Solicitor General of Canada may designate any police force in Canada for the purposes of the enforcement of any of the provisions of this Act that are specified in the designation, subject to any terms and conditions specified in the designation, for any period specified in the designation.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Persons to have powers and duties of officers
Subsection.Text: All members of a police force designated under subsection (1) have the powers and duties of an officer for the purposes of the enforcement of the provisions of this Act specified in the designation.
A designation under subsection (1) or any variation or cancellation of that designation must be published in the Canada Gazette and is not effective before it is so published.

Section.Text: The Minister may designate any person or class of persons as an analyst for the purposes of this Act.

Any officer, if designated by the Minister for the purpose, may administer oaths and take and receive affidavits, declarations and affirmations for the purposes of or incidental to the administration or enforcement of this Act, and every officer so designated has for those purposes all the powers of a commissioner for administering oaths or taking affidavits.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of this Act, authorize any person, whether or not the person is an officer, to make any inquiry that the Minister considers necessary with reference to anything relating to the administration or enforcement of this Act.
If the Minister authorizes a person to make an inquiry, the Minister shall without delay apply to the Tax Court for an order appointing a hearing officer before whom the inquiry will be held.

For the purposes of an inquiry, a hearing officer has all of the powers conferred on a commissioner under sections 4 and 5 of the Inquiries Act and that may be conferred on a commissioner under section 11 of that Act.

A hearing officer shall exercise the powers conferred on a commissioner under section 4 of the Inquiries Act in relation to any person that the person authorized to make the inquiry considers appropriate for the conduct of the inquiry, but the hearing officer shall not exercise the power to punish any person unless, on application by the hearing officer, a judge certifies that the power may be exercised in the matter disclosed in the application and the applicant has given to the person in respect of whom the power is proposed to be exercised 24 hours notice of the hearing of the application, or any shorter notice that the judge considers reasonable.
Any person who gives evidence in an inquiry is entitled to be represented by counsel and, on request made by the person to the Minister, to receive a transcript of that evidence.

Any person whose affairs are investigated in the course of an inquiry is entitled to be present and to be represented by counsel throughout the inquiry unless the hearing officer, on application by the Minister or a person giving evidence, orders otherwise in relation to the whole or any part of the inquiry, on the ground that the presence of the person and their counsel, or either of them, would be prejudicial to the effective conduct of the inquiry.
Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue to a person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a spirits licence, authorizing the person to produce or package spirits;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a wine licence, authorizing the person to produce or package wine;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a user’s licence, authorizing the person to use bulk alcohol or non-duty-paid packaged alcohol;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a tobacco licence, authorizing the person to manufacture tobacco products; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a tobacco dealer’s licence, authorizing the person to carry on the activity of a tobacco dealer.

Subsection.Text: A person is not entitled to a licence under paragraph (1)(a) or (b) by reason only of having been deemed to have packaged alcohol under section 77 or 82.
A person is not entitled to a licence under paragraph (1)(a) by reason only of having been deemed to have produced spirits under subsection 131(2).

Section.Text: Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue a ferment-on-premises registration to a person authorizing the person to possess at their ferment-on-premises facility bulk wine produced at the premises by an individual and owned by the individual.
Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue a user’s registration authorizing the use of non-duty-paid packaged spirits by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a scientific and research laboratory in receipt annually of aid from the Government of Canada or a province, for scientific purposes;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a university or other post-secondary educational institution recognized by a province, for scientific purposes;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a health care facility, for medicinal and scientific purposes; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a health institution in receipt annually of aid from the Government of Canada or a province, for medicinal and scientific purposes.

Section.Text: Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue an alcohol registration to a person authorizing the person to store or transport bulk alcohol or specially denatured alcohol.

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue a specially denatured alcohol registration to a person authorizing the person to possess and use specially denatured alcohol.
The Minister may impose restrictions on the use of particular grades of specially denatured alcohol.

Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue an excise warehouse licence to a person who is not a retailer of alcohol authorizing the person to possess in their excise warehouse non-duty-paid packaged alcohol or a tobacco product that is not stamped.
Even if they are retailers of alcohol, the following persons may be issued an excise warehouse licence under subsection (1):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an alcohol licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a liquor authority; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any person who supplies goods in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations.

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue a special excise warehouse licence to a person who is authorized by a tobacco licensee to be the only person, other than the licensee, who is entitled to distribute to an accredited representative a tobacco product manufactured by the licensee.
The Minister shall not issue to the same person more than one special excise warehouse licence.
The Minister shall not specify more than one premises of a special excise warehouse licensee as a special excise warehouse.
If a person ceases to be authorized by a tobacco licensee to distribute to an accredited representative a tobacco product manufactured by the tobacco licensee,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person shall immediately return the tobacco product of that licensee that is stored in the person’s special excise warehouse to the excise warehouse of the tobacco licensee; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the tobacco licensee shall immediately notify the Minister in writing that the person has ceased to be so authorized.

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall cancel the special excise warehouse licence of the person if the person is no longer authorized by any tobacco licensee to distribute to an accredited representative a tobacco product.

Section.Text: Subject to the regulations, the Minister may, on application, issue to a person who is licensed under the Customs Act to operate a duty free shop a licence authorizing the person to possess and sell imported manufactured tobacco that is subject to a special duty under section 53.

Section.MarginalNote: Refusal to issue licence or registration

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, for any reason the Minister considers sufficient in the public interest, refuse to issue a licence or registration.
Subject to the regulations, the Minister may amend, suspend, renew, cancel or reinstate any licence or registration.
On issuing a licence or registration, or at any later time, the Minister

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may, subject to the regulations, specify the activities that may be carried on under the licence or registration and the premises where those activities may be carried on;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall, in the case of a spirits licence or a tobacco licence, require security in a form satisfactory to the Minister and in an amount determined in accordance with the regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: may impose any other conditions that the Minister considers appropriate with respect to the carrying on of activities under the licence or registration.

Section.Text: A licensee or registrant shall not carry on any activity under their licence or registration otherwise than in accordance with this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Manufacturing tobacco product without a licence prohibited

Subsection.Text: No person shall, other than in accordance with a tobacco licence issued to the person, manufacture a tobacco product.

A person who, whether for consideration or otherwise, provides or offers to provide in their place of business equipment for use in that place by another person in the manufacture of a tobacco product is deemed to be manufacturing the tobacco product and the other person is deemed not to be manufacturing the tobacco product.
An individual who is not a tobacco licensee may manufacture a tobacco product

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: from packaged raw leaf tobacco or manufactured tobacco on which the duty has been paid, if the product is for their personal use; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: from raw leaf tobacco grown on land on which the individual resides, if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the product is for their personal use or that of the members of their family who reside with the individual and who are 18 years of age or older, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the quantity of product manufactured in any year does not exceed 15 kg for the individual and each member of the individual’s family who resides with the individual and who is 18 years of age or older.

Section.Text: No person shall carry on the activity of a tobacco dealer except in accordance with a tobacco dealer’s licence issued to the person.
No person shall package or stamp any raw leaf tobacco or tobacco product unless the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is a tobacco licensee; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is the importer or owner of the tobacco or product and it has been placed in a sufferance warehouse for the purpose of being stamped.

Subsection.Text: Except as permitted under section 40, no person shall remove raw leaf tobacco or a tobacco product from the premises of a tobacco licensee unless the tobacco or product is packaged and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the product is intended for the duty-paid market, it is stamped; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the product is not intended for the duty-paid market, all tobacco markings that are required under this Act to be printed on or affixed to its container are so printed or affixed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to a tobacco licensee who removes from their premises

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: raw leaf tobacco for return to a tobacco grower, for delivery to another tobacco licensee or for export; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: partially manufactured tobacco for delivery to another tobacco licensee or for export.

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — certain tobacco products for sale, etc.
Section.Text: No person shall purchase or receive for sale a tobacco product

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: from a manufacturer who the person knows, or ought to know, is not a tobacco licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is required under this Act to be packaged and stamped unless it is so packaged and stamped; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that the person knows, or ought to know, is fraudulently stamped.

Section.MarginalNote: Selling, etc., unstamped raw leaf tobacco

Subsection.Text: No person shall dispose of, sell, offer for sale, purchase or have in their possession raw leaf tobacco unless the tobacco is packaged and stamped.
Subsection (1) does not apply to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is a tobacco licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the possession of raw leaf tobacco

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in a customs bonded warehouse or a sufferance warehouse by the licensee of that warehouse, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: by a body established under provincial law for the marketing of raw leaf tobacco grown in the province; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the sale, offer for sale or purchase of raw leaf tobacco by a licensed tobacco dealer.

Section.Text: A tobacco grower does not contravene section 26 or 30 by reason only that the grower deals in or has in their possession raw leaf tobacco

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: grown by the grower on their property for sale to a tobacco licensee or a licensed tobacco dealer or for other disposition to a tobacco licensee, if the tobacco is either on the grower’s property or is being transported by the grower

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in connection with the curing of the tobacco,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for delivery to or return from a tobacco licensee, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: for delivery to or return from a body established under provincial law for the marketing of raw leaf tobacco grown in the province;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: grown by any other person, if the grower operates a tobacco drying kiln on the grower’s property and the grower’s possession is solely for the purpose of curing the tobacco and returning it to the other person immediately after completion of the curing process or exporting it in accordance with paragraph (c); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that is to be exported, if the grower has the written approval of the Minister and complies with any conditions that the Minister considers appropriate.

Section.MarginalNote: Unlawful possession or sale of tobacco products

Subsection.Text: No person shall sell, offer for sale or have in their possession a tobacco product unless it is stamped.
Subsection (1) does not apply to the possession of a tobacco product by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a tobacco licensee at the place of manufacture of the product or at the excise warehouse of the licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of cigars or imported manufactured tobacco, an excise warehouse licensee at the excise warehouse of the licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a special excise warehouse licensee at the special excise warehouse of the licensee, if the tobacco product is one that the licensee is permitted under this Act to distribute;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed person who is transporting the product under prescribed circumstances and conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an imported tobacco product, a customs bonded warehouse licensee or a sufferance warehouse licensee in their warehouse;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of cigars, a duty free shop licensee in their duty free shop;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of imported manufactured tobacco, a duty free shop licensee in their duty free shop if the licensee holds a licence issued under section 22;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: an accredited representative for their personal or official use;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of cigars or imported manufactured tobacco, a person as ships’ stores if the acquisition and possession of the product by that person are in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: an individual who has imported the product for their personal use in quantities not in excess of prescribed limits; or

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: an individual who has manufactured the product in accordance with subsection 25(3).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exceptions — sale or offer for sale
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a tobacco licensee sells or offers to sell a tobacco product that is exported by the licensee in accordance with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a tobacco licensee sells or offers to sell

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a tobacco product to a special excise warehouse licensee, if the product is one that the special excise warehouse licensee is permitted under this Act to distribute,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a tobacco product to an accredited representative for their personal or official use,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: cigars to an excise warehouse licensee for delivery as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: cigars to a duty free shop for sale or offer for sale in accordance with the Customs Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: cigars as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a special excise warehouse licensee sells or offers to sell a tobacco product to an accredited representative for their personal or official use, if the product is one that the licensee is permitted under this Act to distribute;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an excise warehouse licensee sells or offers to sell

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an imported tobacco product that is exported by the licensee in accordance with this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an imported tobacco product to an accredited representative for their personal or official use or to a duty free shop, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: cigars or imported manufactured tobacco as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a duty free shop licensee sells or offers to sell cigars in accordance with the Customs Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a duty free shop licensee who holds a licence issued under section 22 sells or offers to sell imported manufactured tobacco in accordance with the Customs Act;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a customs bonded warehouse licensee sells or offers to sell an imported tobacco product that is exported by the licensee in accordance with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a customs bonded warehouse licensee sells or offers to sell an imported tobacco product

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to an accredited representative for their personal or official use,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to a duty free shop for sale or offer for sale in accordance with the Customs Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a person sells or offers for sale cigars or imported manufactured tobacco as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations.

Section.MarginalNote: No sale or distribution except in original package
Section.Text: No person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sell or offer for sale cigars otherwise than in or from the original package;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sell or offer for sale manufactured tobacco otherwise than in the original package; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: distribute free of charge for advertising purposes any tobacco product otherwise than in or from the original package.

Section.Text: A tobacco licensee who manufactures a tobacco product shall not enter the tobacco product into the duty-paid market unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the product has been packaged by the licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the package has printed on it prescribed information; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the product is stamped at the time of packaging.

Section.MarginalNote: Packaging and stamping of imported tobacco

Subsection.Text: If a tobacco product or raw leaf tobacco is imported, it must, before it is released under the Customs Act for entry into the duty-paid market,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: be packaged in a package that has printed on it prescribed information; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: be stamped.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: partially manufactured tobacco that is imported by a tobacco licensee for further manufacturing by the licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a tobacco product that a tobacco licensee is authorized to import under subsection 41(2);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a tobacco product that is imported by an individual for their personal use in quantities not in excess of prescribed limits; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: raw leaf tobacco that is imported by a tobacco licensee.

Section.Text: The absence of stamping on a tobacco product is notice to all persons that duty has not been paid on the product.
If a tobacco product manufactured in Canada is not stamped by a tobacco licensee, the tobacco licensee shall immediately enter the product into the licensee’s excise warehouse.

Section.MarginalNote: No warehousing of tobacco without markings

Subsection.Text: No person shall enter into an excise warehouse a container of tobacco products unless the container has printed on it or affixed to it tobacco markings and other prescribed information.

Subsection.MarginalNote: No delivery of imported tobacco without markings
Subsection.Text: No person shall deliver a container of imported tobacco products that does not have printed on it or affixed to it tobacco markings and other prescribed information to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a duty free shop for sale or offer for sale in accordance with the Customs Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an accredited representative; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a customs bonded warehouse.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply to a tobacco product of a brand if the brand is not commonly sold in Canada and is prescribed.

Subsection (1) does not apply to cigarettes of a particular type or formulation, manufactured in Canada and exported under a brand that is also applied to cigarettes of a different type or formulation that are manufactured and sold in Canada, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cigarettes of the particular type or formulation exported under that brand are prescribed cigarettes; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: cigarettes of the particular type or formulation have never been sold in Canada under that brand or any other brand.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (4), a cigarette of a particular type or formulation sold under a brand may be considered to be different from another cigarette sold under that brand if it is reasonable to consider them to be different having regard to their physical characteristics before and during consumption.

Section.Text: If an imported tobacco product or imported raw leaf tobacco intended for the duty-paid market is not stamped when it is reported under the Customs Act, it shall be placed in a sufferance warehouse for the purpose of being stamped.

Section.MarginalNote: Removal of raw leaf tobacco or waste tobacco

Subsection.Text: No person other than a tobacco licensee shall remove raw leaf tobacco or tobacco that is waste from the premises of the licensee.
If raw leaf tobacco or tobacco that is waste is removed from the premises of a tobacco licensee, the licensee shall deal with the tobacco in the manner authorized by the Minister.
A tobacco licensee may re-work or destroy a tobacco product in the manner authorized by the Minister.
The Minister may authorize a tobacco licensee to import any tobacco product manufactured in Canada by the licensee for re-working or destruction by the licensee in accordance with subsection (1).
Duty is imposed on tobacco products manufactured in Canada or imported and on imported raw leaf tobacco at the rates set out in Schedule 1 and is payable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of tobacco products manufactured in Canada, by the tobacco licensee who manufactured the tobacco products, at the time they are packaged; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of imported tobacco products or raw leaf tobacco, by the importer, owner or other person who is liable under the Customs Act to pay duty levied under section 20 of the Customs Tariff or who would be liable to pay that duty on the tobacco or products if they were subject to that duty.

Subsection.Text: The following rules apply to partially manufactured tobacco that is imported by a tobacco licensee for further manufacture:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of this Act, the tobacco is deemed to be manufactured in Canada by the licensee; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph (1)(a) applies to the tobacco and paragraph (1)(b) and section 44 do not apply.

Section.Text: In addition to the duty imposed under section 42, duty is imposed on cigars at the rates set out in Schedule 2 and is payable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of cigars manufactured and sold in Canada, by the tobacco licensee who manufactured the cigars, at the time of their delivery to a purchaser; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of imported cigars, by the importer, owner or other person who is liable under the Customs Act to pay duty levied under section 20 of the Customs Tariff or who would be liable to pay that duty on the cigars if they were subject to that duty.

Section.Text: The duties imposed under sections 42 and 43 on imported raw leaf tobacco and tobacco products shall be paid and collected under the Customs Act, and interest and penalties shall be imposed, calculated, paid and collected under that Act, as if the duties were a duty levied under section 20 of the Customs Tariff, and, for those purposes, the Customs Act applies with any modifications that the circumstances require.

Subsection.Text: The duties imposed under sections 42 and 43 are relieved on a tobacco product that is not stamped.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Tobacco imported by an individual for personal use
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to the importation of tobacco products by an individual for their personal use to the extent that the quantity of the products imported exceeds the quantity permitted under Chapter 98 of the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff to be imported without the payment of duties (as defined in Note 4 to that Chapter).

Section.Text: The duty imposed under section 42 is relieved on raw leaf tobacco that is imported by a tobacco licensee for manufacture by the licensee.

Section.MarginalNote: Duty relieved — stamped tobacco imported by individual
Section.Text: The duty imposed under section 42 is relieved on manufactured tobacco imported by an individual for their personal use if it was manufactured in Canada and is stamped.

The duty imposed under paragraph 42(1)(b) is relieved on stamped manufactured tobacco that was manufactured in Canada by a tobacco licensee and that is imported by the licensee for re-working or destruction in accordance with section 41.
No person shall enter into an excise warehouse

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a tobacco product that is stamped; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other tobacco product except in accordance with this Act.

Subsection.Text: The definitions in this subsection apply in this section.

Definition.Text: Canadian manufactured tobacco means manufactured tobacco that is manufactured in Canada but does not include partially manufactured tobacco or foreign brand tobacco.
foreign brand tobacco means manufactured tobacco in respect of which the special duty imposed under section 56 is relieved because of section 58.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (5), each of the following constitutes a category of Canadian manufactured tobacco:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cigarettes;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: tobacco sticks; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks.

Subsection.Text: No person shall remove from an excise warehouse or a special excise warehouse a tobacco product manufactured in Canada.
Subject to the regulations, Canadian manufactured tobacco may be removed from the excise warehouse of the tobacco licensee who manufactured it only if it is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for export by the licensee in accordance with subsection (5) and not for delivery to a foreign duty free shop or as foreign ships’ stores;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to the special excise warehouse of a special excise warehouse licensee, if the special excise warehouse licensee is permitted under this Act to distribute the Canadian manufactured tobacco; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to an accredited representative for their personal or official use.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Maximum quantity permitted to be exported from excise warehouse
Subsection.Text: A tobacco licensee shall not, at a particular time in a calendar year, remove a particular quantity of a category of Canadian manufactured tobacco from the licensee’s excise warehouse for export if the total quantity of that category removed in the year up to that time by the licensee from the warehouse for export, plus the particular quantity, exceeds 1.5% of the total quantity of that category manufactured by the licensee in the preceding calendar year.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Quantities to be excluded for the purposes of subsection (5)
Subsection.Text: In subsection (5), the total quantity of a category of Canadian manufactured tobacco manufactured by a licensee in the preceding calendar year does not include any quantity of that category that was exported by the licensee for delivery to a foreign duty free shop or as foreign ships’ stores.
Subject to the regulations, cigars manufactured in Canada may be removed from the excise warehouse of the tobacco licensee who manufactured them only if they are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for export by the licensee in accordance with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to the special excise warehouse of a special excise warehouse licensee, if the special excise warehouse licensee is permitted under this Act to distribute the cigars;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to an accredited representative for their personal or official use;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to another excise warehouse, if the excise warehouse licensee of the other excise warehouse certifies in the prescribed form to the tobacco licensee that the cigars are for delivery as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to a duty free shop for sale or offer for sale in accordance with the Customs Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception for partially manufactured tobacco or foreign brand tobacco
Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, partially manufactured tobacco or foreign brand tobacco may be removed from the excise warehouse of the tobacco licensee who manufactured it only if it is exported by the licensee and not for delivery to a foreign duty free shop or as foreign ships’ stores.

Subject to the regulations, cigars manufactured in Canada may be removed from an excise warehouse referred to in paragraph (7)(e) for delivery as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Removal from warehouse for reworking or destruction
Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, tobacco products manufactured in Canada may be removed from the excise warehouse of the tobacco licensee who manufactured them if they are removed for reworking or destruction by the licensee in accordance with section 41.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Removal from special excise warehouse — accredited representatives
Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, Canadian manufactured tobacco and cigars may be removed from a special excise warehouse for delivery to an accredited representative for their personal or official use if the special excise warehouse licensee is permitted under this Act to distribute the tobacco or cigars.
No person shall remove an imported tobacco product from an excise warehouse.
Subject to the regulations, an imported tobacco product may be removed from an excise warehouse

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to another excise warehouse;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to an accredited representative for their personal or official use;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery as ships’ stores, in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for delivery to a duty free shop for sale or offer for sale in accordance with the Customs Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for export in accordance with this Act by the excise warehouse licensee.

Section.Text: No special excise warehouse licensee shall store a tobacco product that is manufactured in Canada in their special excise warehouse for any purpose other than its sale and distribution to an accredited representative for the personal or official use of the representative.

Section.MarginalNote: Special duty on imported manufactured tobacco delivered to duty free shop

Subsection.Text: A special duty is imposed on imported manufactured tobacco that is delivered to a duty free shop at the rates set out in section 1 of Schedule 3.

Subsection.MarginalNote: When and by whom duty is payable
Subsection.Text: The special duty is payable at the time of delivery and is payable by the duty free shop licensee.
In this section, traveller’s tobacco means manufactured tobacco that is imported by a person at any time and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is classified under tariff item No. 9804.10.00, 9804.20.00, 9805.00.00 or 9807.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: would be classified under tariff item No. 9804.10.00 or 9804.20.00 in that List but for the fact that the total value for duty as determined under section 46 of the Customs Act of all goods imported by the person at that time exceeds the maximum value specified in that tariff item.

Subsection.Text: A special duty is imposed on traveller’s tobacco at the time it is imported at the rates set out in section 2 of Schedule 3.

Subsection.MarginalNote: When and how duty is payable
Subsection.Text: The special duty shall be paid and collected under the Customs Act, and interest and penalties shall be imposed, calculated, paid and collected under that Act, as if the special duty were a duty levied under section 20 of the Customs Tariff, and for those purposes, the Customs Act applies with any modifications that the circumstances require.
The special duty is not imposed on traveller’s tobacco imported by an individual for their personal use if it was manufactured in Canada and is stamped.

Section.Text: In sections 56 to 58, tobacco product means manufactured tobacco other than partially manufactured tobacco.

Subsection.Text: A special duty is imposed on a tobacco product that is manufactured in Canada and exported

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the export is in accordance with paragraph 50(4)(a) by the tobacco licensee who manufactured it, at the rates set out in section 3 of Schedule 3; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, at the rates set out in section 4 of Schedule 3.

Subsection.MarginalNote: When and by whom duty is payable
Subsection.Text: Subject to sections 57 and 58, the special duty is payable, at the time the tobacco product is exported, by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph (1)(a) applies, the tobacco licensee who manufactured it; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph (1)(b) applies, the person who exported it.

Section.MarginalNote: Duty relieved — deliveries to a foreign duty free shop and as foreign ships’ stores
Section.Text: The special duty imposed under section 56 is relieved on a tobacco product that is exported by the tobacco licensee who manufactured it for delivery to a foreign duty free shop or as foreign ships’ stores.

Subsection.Text: The special duty imposed under section 56 is relieved on a tobacco product of a particular brand if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the tobacco product of that brand is prescribed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: during the three-year period before the year in which the tobacco product of that brand is exported, the tobacco product of that brand was not sold in Canada, other than in a duty free shop, except in quantities not significantly greater than the minimum quantities sufficient for the purposes of registering the trade mark for that brand; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: during any year before the three-year period referred to in paragraph (b), sales in Canada of the product of that brand never exceeded

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: 0.5% of total sales in Canada of similar products, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if another percentage that is less than 0.5% is prescribed for the purposes of this subsection, that percentage of total sales in Canada of similar products.

Subsection.Text: The special duty imposed under section 56 is relieved on cigarettes of a particular type or formulation manufactured in Canada and exported under a brand that is also applied to cigarettes of a different type or formulation that are manufactured and sold in Canada if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cigarettes of the particular type or formulation exported under that brand are prescribed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: cigarettes of the particular type or formulation have never been sold in Canada under that brand or any other brand.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (2), a cigarette of a particular type or formulation sold under a brand may be considered to be different from another cigarette sold under that brand if it is reasonable to consider them to be different having regard to their physical characteristics before and during consumption.

Section.MarginalNote: Application — Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act
Section.Text: For greater certainty, the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act continues to apply to the importation, sending, taking and transportation of intoxicating liquor into a province.

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — production and packaging of spirits

Subsection.Text: No person shall, except in accordance with a spirits licence issued to the person, produce or package spirits.
Subsection (1) does not apply to the packaging of spirits from a marked special container by a purchaser at a bottle-your-own premises.

Section.Text: No person shall possess a still or other equipment suitable for the production of spirits with the intent of producing spirits unless the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is a spirits licensee; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has a pending application for a spirits licence.

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — production and packaging of wine

Subsection.Text: No person shall, except in accordance with a wine licence issued to the person, produce or package wine.
Subsection (1) does not apply to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the production of wine by an individual for their personal use;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the packaging of wine referred to in paragraph (a) by an individual for their personal use; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the packaging of wine from a marked special container by a purchaser at a bottle-your-own premises.

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — sale of wine produced for personal use
Section.Text: No person shall sell or put to a commercial use wine that was produced, or produced and packaged, by an individual for their personal use.
For the purposes of this Act, wine is not produced or packaged by an individual if it has been produced or packaged by a person acting on their behalf.

No person shall carry on at a ferment-on-premises facility any activity specified in a licence or registration issued under this Act other than an activity specified in a ferment-on-premises registration.

Sections 67 to 72, 74, 76, 80, 85, 88, 97 to 100 and 102 do not apply to imported alcohol or specially denatured alcohol that is, in accordance with the Customs Act, the Customs Tariff and the regulations made under those Acts,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: transported by a customs bonded carrier from a place outside Canada to another place outside Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: stored in a customs bonded warehouse or sufferance warehouse for subsequent delivery to a place outside Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: transported by a customs bonded carrier

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: from a place outside Canada to a customs bonded warehouse or a sufferance warehouse for subsequent delivery to a place outside Canada, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: from a customs bonded warehouse or a sufferance warehouse to a place outside Canada.

Section.Text: No person shall sell

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: bulk alcohol unless it was produced or imported in accordance with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: packaged alcohol unless it was, in accordance with this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: produced and packaged in Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: imported and packaged in Canada, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: imported; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a marked special container of alcohol unless it was marked in accordance with this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Availability and sampling of imported DA and SDA

Subsection.Text: Any person who imports a product that is reported under the Customs Act as being denatured alcohol or specially denatured alcohol shall make the product available for sampling and the product is required to be sampled by the Minister before it is released under that Act.
The samples must be tested to verify that the product is denatured alcohol or specially denatured alcohol.
The Minister may at any time waive the requirement to sample an imported product under subsection (1).

The Minister may fix fees to be paid by the importer of the product but those fees must not exceed an amount determined by the Minister to be the costs to Her Majesty in respect of the sampling and testing.

Section.Text: No person shall own bulk alcohol unless it was produced or imported in accordance with this Act.

Subsection.Text: No person shall possess bulk alcohol.
Subsection (1) does not apply

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to a spirits licensee or a licensed user who possesses bulk spirits that were produced or imported by a spirits licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to a wine licensee or a licensed user who possesses bulk wine that was produced or imported by a wine licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to a licensed user who possesses bulk alcohol that was imported by the licensed user;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to an alcohol registrant who possesses, for the purposes of storage or transportation, bulk alcohol that was produced by an alcohol licensee or imported by an alcohol licensee or licensed user;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: to a sufferance warehouse licensee who possesses in their sufferance warehouse bulk alcohol that was imported by a person permitted to do so under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: to a ferment-on-premises registrant who possesses bulk wine that was produced at the registrant’s ferment-on-premises facility by an individual for the individual’s personal use; or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: to an individual who possesses less than 500 L of bulk wine that was lawfully produced at a residence or a ferment-on-premises facility for an individual’s personal use.

Section.Text: No person shall give possession of bulk spirits to a person other than a spirits licensee, a licensed user or an alcohol registrant.

Subsection.Text: No person shall give possession of bulk wine to a person other than a wine licensee, a licensed user or an alcohol registrant.
Subsection (1) does not apply to an individual who, in connection with their personal use of it, gives possession of bulk wine lawfully produced by an individual for their personal use.

Section.Text: A licensed user shall not use or dispose of bulk alcohol other than to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use it in an approved formulation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: use it in a process in which the absolute ethyl alcohol is destroyed to the extent approved by the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: use it to produce vinegar;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: use it in accordance with section 130 or 131;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: return it to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of alcohol returned in circumstances to which paragraph 105(1)(a) or 114(1)(a) applies, a licensee referred to in that paragraph, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, the alcohol licensee who supplied it;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subject to section 76, export it;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: use it for analysis in a manner approved by the Minister; or

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: destroy it in a manner approved by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: No person shall import bulk spirits other than a spirits licensee, a licensed user or, if the spirits are in a special container, an excise warehouse licensee in accordance with section 80.
No person shall import bulk wine other than a wine licensee, a licensed user or, if the wine is in a special container, an excise warehouse licensee in accordance with section 85.

Section.Text: If bulk alcohol is imported under circumstances in which subsection 3(1) of the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act applies, the alcohol is deemed, for the purposes of this Act, to have been imported by the person who would have been the importer in the absence of that subsection and not by Her Majesty in right of a province or a liquor authority.
No person shall export bulk alcohol other than

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the alcohol licensee who is responsible for the alcohol;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the licensed user who imported the alcohol; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a person required to do so under section 101.

Section.Text: If a special container of spirits is marked, the spirits are deemed to be packaged at the time the container is marked.

Subsection.Text: No person shall mark a special container of spirits unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is a spirits licensee; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the container is one that has been placed in a sufferance warehouse in accordance with section 80 and the person marks the container in the circumstances described in that section.

Subsection.Text: If a spirits licensee marks a special container of spirits, the licensee shall immediately enter it into an excise warehouse.

Section.Text: No person, other than an excise warehouse licensee, shall import a marked special container of spirits.

If a special container of spirits that is imported by an excise warehouse licensee is not marked when it is reported under the Customs Act, it shall be placed in a sufferance warehouse for the purpose of being marked.
If a marked special container of spirits is released under the Customs Act, the excise warehouse licensee who imported it shall immediately enter it into their excise warehouse.
If a special container of wine is marked, the wine is deemed to be packaged at the time the container is marked.

Subsection.Text: No person shall mark a special container of wine unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is a wine licensee; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the container is one that has been placed in a sufferance warehouse in accordance with section 85 and the person marks the container in the circumstances described in that section.

Subsection.Text: If a wine licensee marks a special container of wine, the licensee shall immediately enter it into an excise warehouse.

Section.Text: No person, other than an excise warehouse licensee, shall import a marked special container of wine.

If a special container of wine that is imported by an excise warehouse licensee is not marked when it is reported under the Customs Act, it shall be placed in a sufferance warehouse for the purpose of being marked.
If a marked special container of wine is released under the Customs Act, the excise warehouse licensee who imported it shall immediately enter it into their excise warehouse.
Every alcohol licensee who packages alcohol shall cause all prescribed information to be displayed on the container containing the alcohol and on any packaging encasing the container

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of wine that is entered into an excise warehouse immediately after it is packaged, before the wine is removed from the warehouse; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, immediately after the alcohol is packaged.

Subsection.Text: No person shall possess non-duty-paid packaged alcohol.
Non-duty-paid packaged alcohol, other than alcohol in a marked special container,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is packaged by an alcohol licensee or imported by an excise warehouse licensee may be possessed by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an excise warehouse licensee in their excise warehouse,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a licensed user in their specified premises,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered user for use in accordance with their registration,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a prescribed person who is transporting the alcohol under prescribed circumstances and conditions,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a duty free shop licensee, in their duty free shop,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: an accredited representative, for their personal or official use, or

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person as ships’ stores, if the acquisition and possession of the alcohol by that person are in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is imported may be possessed by a sufferance warehouse licensee, in their sufferance warehouse;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that is imported by a licensed user may be possessed by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the licensed user in their specified premises, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a prescribed person who is transporting the alcohol under prescribed circumstances and conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: that is imported by an accredited representative may be possessed by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the accredited representative, for their personal or official use, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a prescribed person who is transporting the alcohol under prescribed circumstances and conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that is imported for sale in a duty free shop, for sale to an accredited representative or for use as ships’ stores, may be possessed by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a customs bonded warehouse licensee, in their customs bonded warehouse,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a duty free shop licensee, in their duty free shop,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an accredited representative, for their personal or official use,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a customs bonded carrier in accordance with the Customs Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a person as ships’ stores, if the acquisition and possession of the alcohol by that person are in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: that is imported for supply to an air carrier that is licensed under section 69 or 73 of the Canada Transportation Act to operate an international air service may be possessed by a customs bonded warehouse licensee, in their customs bonded warehouse;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: that is imported by an individual in accordance with the Customs Act and the Customs Tariff for their personal use may be possessed by an individual; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: that is wine that is produced and packaged by an individual for their personal use may be possessed by an individual.

Subsection.Text: A non-duty-paid marked special container of alcohol may be possessed by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an excise warehouse licensee in their excise warehouse;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed person who is transporting the alcohol under prescribed circumstances and conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an imported special container of alcohol, a sufferance warehouse licensee in their sufferance warehouse; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a special container of spirits that is marked for delivery to and use by a registered user, a registered user for use in accordance with their registration.

Section.Text: A ferment-on-premises registrant shall not store packaged wine at their ferment-on-premises facility.
A licensed user shall not use or dispose of non-duty-paid packaged alcohol other than to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use it in an approved formulation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: use it in a process in which the absolute ethyl alcohol is destroyed to the extent approved by the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: use it to produce vinegar;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: return it under prescribed conditions to the excise warehouse licensee who supplied it;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: export it, if the alcohol was imported by the licensed user;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: use it for analysis in a manner approved by the Minister; or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: destroy it in a manner approved by the Minister.

Section.Text: A registered user shall not use or dispose of non-duty-paid packaged spirits other than to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use them in accordance with their registration;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: use them for analysis in a manner approved by the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: return them under prescribed conditions to the excise warehouse licensee who supplied them; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: destroy them in a manner approved by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: No person shall remove spirits from a marked special container of spirits other than

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a registered user, in the case of a container that is marked for delivery to and use by a registered user; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a purchaser of the spirits at a bottle-your-own premises, in the case of a container that is marked for delivery to and use at a bottle-your-own premises.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Removal of spirits from returned container
