Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the fees or the manner of determining any fees to be paid for a licence or registration;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: requiring any class of persons to make returns respecting any class of information required in connection with the administration or enforcement of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: requiring any person to provide the Minister with the person’s Social Insurance Number;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the sale under section 266 of alcohol, tobacco products, raw leaf tobacco or specially denatured alcohol seized under section 260;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing any matter or thing that by this Act is to be or may be prescribed; and

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: generally to carry out the purposes and provisions of this Act.

Subsection.Text: A regulation made under this Act has effect from the day it is published in the Canada Gazette or at any later time that may be specified in the regulation, unless it provides otherwise and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has a relieving effect only;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: corrects an ambiguous or deficient enactment that was not in accordance with the objects of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is consequential on an amendment to this Act that is applicable before the day on which the regulation is published in the Canada Gazette; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: gives effect to a budgetary or other public announcement, in which case the regulation shall not, unless paragraph (a), (b) or (c) applies, have effect before the day on which the announcement was made.

Heading.Label: PART 8
Heading.TitleText: TRANSITIONAL PROVISIONS AND CONSEQUENTIAL, RELATED AND COORDINATING AMENDMENTS

Section.Text: In sections 306 to 320, implementation date means the day on which Parts 3 and 4 come into force.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional treatment of duties on packaged spirits
Section.Text: The following rules apply to packaged spirits on which a duty, at a rate determined by the application of section 1 of Part I of the schedule to the Excise Act, was imposed under that Act or levied under the Customs Tariff but that had not become payable before the implementation date:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as of that day, the duty is relieved;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: as of that day, the Excise Act ceases to apply in respect of the spirits;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of imported packaged spirits that have not been released under the Customs Act, this Act, the Customs Act and the Customs Tariff apply in respect of them as though they were imported on that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any other packaged spirits, this Act applies in respect of them as though

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: they were produced and packaged in Canada on that day by the person having possession of them immediately before that day and the person were permitted under this Act to produce and package them, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the spirits are in the possession of a duty free shop or an accredited representative or delivered as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations, they had been entered into an excise warehouse and then removed from the warehouse in accordance with paragraph 147(1)(a) on that day.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional treatment of duties on bulk spirits

Subsection.Text: The following rules apply to bulk spirits on which a duty, at a rate determined by the application of section 1 of Part I of the schedule to the Excise Act, was imposed under that Act or levied under the Customs Tariff but that had not become payable before the implementation date:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as of that day, the duty is relieved;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: as of that day, the Excise Act ceases to apply in respect of the spirits;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of imported bulk spirits that have not been released under the Customs Act, this Act, the Customs Act and the Customs Tariff apply in respect of them as though they were imported on that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any other bulk spirits, this Act applies in respect of them as though they were produced in Canada on that day by the person having possession of them immediately before that day.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transitional treatment of bulk spirits imported for bottling or blending
Subsection.Text: The following rules apply to bulk spirits on which a duty, at a rate determined by the application of section 1 of Part I of the schedule to the Excise Act, was levied under the Customs Tariff and remitted under the Distilled Spirits for Bottling in Bond Remission Order or the Imported Spirits for Blending Remission Order before the implementation date:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as of that day, the duty imposed on the spirits under subsection 135(1) of the Excise Act when they were entered into a distillery is relieved;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: as of that day, the Excise Act ceases to apply in respect of the spirits; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: this Act applies in respect of them as though they were produced in Canada on that day by the person having possession of them immediately before that day.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional treatment of excise taxes on wine
Section.Text: The following rules apply to wine on which tax was imposed under section 27 of the Excise Tax Act but had not become payable before the implementation date:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as of that day, the tax is relieved;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: as of that day, Parts III, VI and VII of the Excise Tax Act cease to apply in respect of the wine;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of imported wine that has not been released under the Customs Act, this Act, the Customs Act and the Customs Tariff apply in respect of the wine as though it were imported on that day;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of bulk wine to which paragraph (c) does not apply, this Act applies in respect of it as though it were produced in Canada on that day

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the wine is located in a ferment-on-premises facility or at the residence of an individual, by the individual who owned the wine immediately before that day, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, by the person having possession of it immediately before that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of wine to which neither paragraph (c) nor (d) apply, this Act applies in respect of it as though

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it were produced and packaged in Canada on that day by the person having possession of it immediately before that day and the person were permitted under this Act to produce and package it, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of wine in the possession of a duty free shop or an accredited representative or delivered as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations, it had been entered into an excise warehouse and then removed from the warehouse in accordance with paragraph 147(1)(a) on that day.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional treatment of packaged wine in inventory of small manufacturers

Subsection.Text: If tax under Part IV of the Excise Tax Act is not payable in respect of packaged wine because it was produced by a person who is exempt from payment of excise tax under the Small Manufacturers or Producers Exemption Regulations, subsection 135(1) does not apply to the wine if it was packaged before the implementation date.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Determination of sales for transitional purposes
Subsection.Text: For the period beginning on the implementation date and ending on the day that is one year after that day, the words “products subject to duty under subsection (1), or would have been so subject to duty in the absence of this subsection,” in paragraph 135(2)(b) shall be read as “goods referred to in paragraph 2(1)(a) of the Small Manufacturers or Producers Exemption Regulations”.

Section.MarginalNote: Application of Act to tax-paid packaged wine

Subsection.Text: If packaged wine on which tax imposed under section 27 of the Excise Tax Act became payable before the implementation date is entered into the excise warehouse of an excise warehouse licensee on or before the end of six months after that day, this Act applies in respect of the wine as though it were produced and packaged in Canada by the licensee and the licensee were permitted under this Act to produce and package it on the day it is entered into the warehouse.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Refund of excise tax paid on packaged wine
Subsection.Text: If the tax imposed under section 27 of the Excise Tax Act in respect of the wine entered into the warehouse has been paid, the licensee may apply to the Minister for a refund of the tax.
No refund shall be paid under this section unless the application for the refund is filed with the Minister in the prescribed form and manner within one year after the implementation date.

Section.MarginalNote: Application of Act to tax-paid bulk wine

Subsection.Text: If bulk wine on which tax imposed under section 27 of the Excise Tax Act became payable before the implementation date is entered into the specified premises of a licensed user on that day, this Act applies in respect of the wine as though it were produced in Canada on that day by the user and the user were permitted to produce the wine.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Refund of excise tax paid on bulk wine
Subsection.Text: If the tax imposed under section 27 of the Excise Tax Act in respect of the wine entered into the specified premises of a licensed user has been paid, the user may apply to the Minister for a refund of the tax.
No refund shall be paid under this section unless the application for the refund is filed with the Minister in the prescribed form and manner within one year after the implementation date.
The definitions in this subsection apply in this section.

Definition.Text: bonded manufacturer means a person who holds, before the implementation date, a licence under subsection 182(1) of the Excise Act.
licensed pharmacist means a person who holds, before the implementation date, a licence under subsection 136(2) of the Excise Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Application of Act to spirits in possession of bonded manufacturer or licensed pharmacist
Subsection.Text: If, on the implementation date, a bonded manufacturer or a licensed pharmacist possesses, in accordance with their licence, spirits that were produced before that day, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as of that day, the Excise Act ceases to apply in respect of the spirits; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: this Act applies in respect of them as though

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of bulk spirits, the spirits were produced in Canada on that day by the manufacturer or the pharmacist and, if they are a licensed user, they were permitted to produce the spirits, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of packaged spirits, the spirits were produced and packaged in Canada on that day by the manufacturer or the pharmacist and they were permitted to produce and package the spirits.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Refund of duty paid by bonded manufacturer or licensed pharmacist
Subsection.Text: If, on the implementation date, a bonded manufacturer or licensed pharmacist possesses spirits on which duty at a rate determined by the application of subsection 1(2) or (3) of Part I of the schedule to the Excise Act was paid, the manufacturer or pharmacist may apply to the Minister for a refund of the duty.
No refund shall be paid under this section unless the application for the refund is filed with the Minister in the prescribed form and manner within one year after the implementation date.

Section.MarginalNote: Application of Act to spirits to be used for scientific purposes
Section.Text: If a person described by any of paragraphs 135(2)(a) to (d) of the Excise Act possesses, on the implementation date, spirits in respect of which a drawback under subsection 135(2) of that Act is granted at any time, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as of that day, the Excise Act ceases to apply in respect of the spirits;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: this Act applies in respect of them as though

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of bulk spirits, the spirits were produced in Canada on that day by the person and, if the person is a registered user, the person were permitted to produce the spirits, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of packaged spirits,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the spirits were produced and packaged in Canada on that day by the person,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the person were permitted to produce and package the spirits, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: if the person is a registered user, the spirits were, on that day, entered into an excise warehouse and then removed from the warehouse in accordance with subparagraph 147(1)(a)(iii); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the spirits are contained in a special container and the person is a registered user

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person shall, despite subsection 78(1), mark the container on that day, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the container is deemed to have been entered into an excise warehouse and then removed from the warehouse in accordance with paragraph 147(2)(a) on that day.

Section.MarginalNote: Application of Act to alcohol in bottle-your-own premises
Section.Text: The following rules apply to alcohol contained in a special container located on a person’s bottle-your-own premises on the implementation date:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person shall, despite subsections 78(1) and 83(1), mark the container on that day;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of spirits, this Act applies in respect of them as though the duty, at a rate determined by the application of section 1 of Part I of the schedule to the Excise Act, that had become payable before that day in respect of them were imposed and, if the duty is paid, paid under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of wine,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 135(1), section 82 does not apply to the marking of the container under paragraph (a), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: this Act applies in respect of the wine as though the tax under section 27 of the Excise Tax Act that had become payable before that day in respect of it were a duty that was imposed and, if the tax is paid, paid under this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Removal of alcohol from customs bonded warehouse

Subsection.Text: If packaged alcohol is located in a customs bonded warehouse on the implementation date,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the alcohol shall be removed from the warehouse; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any duty on the alcohol that is imposed under this Act or levied under section 21.2 of the Customs Tariff as a result of the operation of section 306 or 308 is payable on that day unless the alcohol is without delay entered into an excise warehouse.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply if the alcohol in the customs bonded warehouse is to be

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: exported in accordance with this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: delivered

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to an accredited representative for their personal or official use,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to a duty free shop for sale in accordance with the Customs Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: to an air carrier that is licensed under section 69 or 73 of the Canada Transportation Act to operate an international air service.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional treatment of Canadian manufactured tobacco products

Subsection.Text: The following rules apply to a tobacco product manufactured in Canada before the implementation date:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if tax on the product imposed under section 23 of the Excise Tax Act had not become payable before that day,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the tax is relieved,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if duty on the product imposed under the Excise Act had not become payable before that day, the duty is relieved, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: this Act applies in respect of the product as though it were manufactured in Canada on that day by the manufacturer to the same extent that the product was manufactured immediately before that day;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the product was stamped or marked under the Excise Act, the product is deemed to be stamped or marked, as the case may be, under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Excise Act and Parts III, VI and VII of the Excise Tax Act cease to apply in respect of the product.

Subsection.Text: If duty imposed under the Excise Act on a tobacco product manufactured in Canada before the implementation date had become payable before that day but tax under section 23 of the Excise Tax Act had not become payable before that day, the manufacturer of the product may apply to the Minister for a refund of the duty.
No refund shall be paid under this section unless the application for the refund is filed with the Minister in the prescribed form and manner within one year after the implementation date.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional treatment of imported tobacco products
Section.Text: The following rules apply to an imported tobacco product:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if duty levied under section 21 of the Customs Tariff and tax imposed under section 23 of the Excise Tax Act on the product had not become payable before the implementation date,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the duty and tax are relieved, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: this Act and the Customs Act apply in respect of the product as though it were imported into Canada on that day by the importer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the product was stamped or marked under the Excise Act, the product is deemed to be stamped or marked, as the case may be, under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Excise Act and Parts III, VI and VII of the Excise Tax Act cease to apply in respect of the product.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional treatment of imported raw leaf tobacco
Section.Text: If, on the implementation date, a person possesses raw leaf tobacco that was imported before that day, this Act applies as though the person imported the tobacco on that day.

Section.MarginalNote: Removal of cigars from customs bonded warehouse
Section.Text: If cigars manufactured in Canada are located in a customs bonded warehouse on the implementation date, they shall be removed from the warehouse and entered into an excise warehouse on that day.

Section.MarginalNote: Removal of tobacco products from bonding warehouse of manufacturer

Subsection.Text: If a tobacco product manufactured in Canada is, on the implementation date, located in a bonding warehouse of a person who is licensed under subsection 196(1) of the Excise Act, the product shall be removed from the warehouse and entered into an excise warehouse on that day.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Removal of tobacco products from bonding warehouse of authorized distributor
Subsection.Text: If a tobacco product manufactured in Canada is, on the implementation date, located in a bonding warehouse of a person who is licensed under paragraph 50(1)(c) of the Excise Act, the product shall, on that day, be removed from the warehouse and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: entered into the person’s special excise warehouse, if the person is a special excise warehouse licensee and the product is one that the person is permitted under this Act to distribute; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: returned to the excise warehouse of the tobacco licensee who manufactured the product.

Subsection.Text: The definition tobacco product in subsection 23(1) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2000 is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: tobacco product has the meaning assigned by section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition alcoholic beverage in subsection 23(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: wine, within the meaning assigned by section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;

Heading.TitleText: Canada Customs and Revenue Agency Act

Section.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition program legislation in section 2 of the Canada Customs and Revenue Agency Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the Governor in Council or Parliament authorizes the Minister, the Agency, the Commissioner or an employee of the Agency to administer or enforce, including the Customs Act, the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act and the Special Import Measures Act; or

Section.Text: Section 7 of the Act is replaced the following:

The Minister may designate any person, or person within a class of persons, as an officer as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act, section 2 of the Excise Act or section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001 to exercise any powers or perform any duties and functions of an officer under those Acts that the Minister may specify.
Paragraph (d) of the definition peace officer in section 2 of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an officer or a person having the powers of a customs or excise officer when performing any duty in the administration of the Customs Act, the Excise Act or the Excise Act, 2001,

Section.MarginalNote: R.S., c. 1 (2nd Supp.), s. 213(3) (Sch. III, s. 1)
Section.Text: Subsection 78(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, civil aircraft means all aircraft other than aircraft operated by the Canadian Forces, a police force in Canada or persons engaged in the administration or enforcement of the Customs Act, the Excise Act or the Excise Act, 2001.

Section.Text: The reference to “section 126.1 (possession of property obtained by excise offences), 126.2 (laundering proceeds of excise offences), 158 (unlawful distillation of spirits) or 163 (unlawful selling of spirits) or subsection 233(1) (unlawful packaging or stamping) or 240(1) (unlawful possession or sale of manufactured tobacco or cigars) of the Excise Act” in the definition offence in section 183 of the Act is replaced by a reference to “section 214 (unlawful production, sale, etc., of tobacco or alcohol), 216 (unlawful possession of tobacco product), 218 (unlawful possession, sale, etc., of alcohol), 219 (falsifying or destroying records), 230 (possession of property obtained by excise offences) or 231 (laundering proceeds of excise offences) of the Excise Act, 2001”.
Paragraph (b.1) of the definition enterprise crime offence in section 462.3 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: an offence against section 214, 216, 218, 230 or 231 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 153, 159, 163.1 or 163.2 of the Customs Act or subsection 52.1(9) of the Competition Act, or

Subsection.Text: The definitions cigar and manufactured tobacco in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act are repealed.
The definitions duties and tobacco product in subsection 2(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: duties means any duties or taxes levied or imposed on imported goods under the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act, the Special Import Measures Act or any other Act of Parliament, but, for the purposes of subsection 3(1), paragraphs 59(3)(b) and 65(1)(b), sections 69 and 73 and subsections 74(1), 75(2) and 76(1), does not include taxes imposed under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act;
tobacco product has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition designated goods in subsection 2(1) of the Act is repealed.
The definition designated goods in subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (i):

Paragraph.Label: (i.1)
Paragraph.Text: spirits,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: raw leaf tobacco has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
specially denatured alcohol has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
spirits has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
spirits licensee has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
tobacco licensee has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
wine has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
wine licensee has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001.

Subsection.Text: The definitions alcohol, ethyl alcohol and spirits and wine in subsection 2(1.1) of the Act are repealed.

Section.Text: Subsection 3(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: All duties or taxes levied on imported goods under the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act, the Special Import Measures Act or any other law relating to customs are binding on Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province in respect of any goods imported by or on behalf of Her Majesty.

Section.Text: Paragraph 24(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: as a duty free shop for the sale of goods free of certain duties or taxes levied on goods under the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act, the Special Import Measures Act or any other law relating to customs, to persons who are about to leave Canada

Section.Text: Subsection 26(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In subsection (1), duties means duties or taxes levied under the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act, the Special Import Measures Act or any other law relating to customs.
The portion of subsection 28(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The operator of a sufferance warehouse, bonded warehouse or duty free shop is liable for all duties or taxes levied under the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act, the Special Import Measures Act or any other law relating to customs on goods that have been received in the warehouse or duty free shop unless the operator proves that the goods
Subsections 28(1.1) and (1.2) of the Act are repealed.
The portion of subsection 28(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The rates of duties or taxes payable on goods under subsection (1) shall
Subsection 28(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Definition of duties not to apply
Subsection.Text: The definition duties in subsection 2(1) does not apply for the purposes of subsections (1) and (2).

Section.Text: Subsection 32.2(8) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a declaration of tariff classification is rendered incorrect by a failure referred to in subsection (6), for the purposes of paragraph (2)(b), duties do not include duties or taxes levied under the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act or the Special Import Measures Act.

Section.Text: Section 33 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Release prior to payment of duties

Subsection.Text: In prescribed circumstances, goods may be released prior to the payment of duties levied on them.
If goods are released under this section, the person who accounted for the goods under subsection 32(2) or (3) shall pay the duties levied on them within the prescribed time.
In subsection (2), duties does not include the duties levied under

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 21.1(1) of the Customs Tariff, if they are paid and collected in accordance with subsection 21.1(2) of that Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 21.2(1) and (2) of the Customs Tariff, if they are paid and collected in accordance with subsection 21.2(3) of that Act.

Section.Text: Section 44 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If duties, other than duties or taxes levied under the Excise Act, 2001 or the Excise Tax Act, are imposed on goods at a percentage rate, such duties shall be calculated by applying the rate to a value determined in accordance with sections 45 to 55.
Clause 48(5)(b)(ii)(B) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: any duties and taxes paid or payable by reason of the importation of the goods or sale of the goods in Canada, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, any duties or taxes levied on the goods under the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act, the Special Import Measures Act or any other law relating to customs; and

Section.Text: Subsection 74(1.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The duties that may be refunded under paragraph (1)(f) do not include duties or taxes levied under the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act or the Special Import Measures Act.

Section.Text: Section 117 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 117(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), if spirits, wine, specially denatured alcohol, raw leaf tobacco or tobacco products are seized under this Act, they shall not be returned to the person from whom they were seized or any other person unless they were seized in error.

Section.Text: Section 119.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, the sale under subsection (1) of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: spirits or specially denatured alcohol may only be to a spirits licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: wine may only be to a wine licensee; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: raw leaf tobacco or a tobacco product may only be to a tobacco licensee.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 142(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Disposal of things abandoned or forfeit

Subsection.Text: Unless the thing is spirits, specially denatured alcohol, wine, raw leaf tobacco or a tobacco product, anything that has been abandoned to Her Majesty in right of Canada under this Act and anything the forfeiture of which is final under this Act shall

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 142:

Section.MarginalNote: Dealing with abandoned or forfeited alcohol, etc.

Subsection.Text: If spirits, specially denatured alcohol, wine, raw leaf tobacco or a tobacco product is abandoned or finally forfeited under this Act, the Minister may sell, destroy or otherwise deal with it.
Subject to the regulations, the sale under subsection (1) of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: spirits or specially denatured alcohol may only be to a spirits licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: wine may only be to a wine licensee; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: raw leaf tobacco or a tobacco product may only be to a tobacco licensee.

Section.Text: Paragraph 163.1(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the commission of an offence contrary to section 153 or under section 159, in relation to spirits, wine or tobacco products, or under section 163.2; or

Section.Text: Paragraph 163.2(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the commission of an offence contrary to section 153 or under section 159, in relation to spirits, wine or tobacco products; or

Section.Text: Subsection 163.3(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Part XII.2 of Criminal Code applicable

Subsection.Text: Sections 462.3 and 462.32 to 462.5 of the Criminal Code apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, in respect of proceedings for an offence contrary to section 153 or under section 159, in relation to spirits, wine or tobacco products, or under section 163.1 or 163.2.

Heading.TitleText: Customs and Excise Offshore Application Act

Section.MarginalNote: R.S., c. 1 (2nd Supp.), s. 213(3) (Sch. III, s. 2)
Section.Text: The portion of the definition federal customs laws in subsection 2(1) of the Customs and Excise Offshore Application Act after paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedDefinition.Text: that relate to customs or excise, whether those Acts, regulations or rules come into force before or after June 30, 1983 and, for greater certainty but without restricting the generality of the foregoing, includes the following Acts, namely, the Customs Act, the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act, the Export and Import Permits Act, the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act and the Special Import Measures Act;

Section.Text: Section 21 of the Customs Tariff is replaced by the following:
The definitions in this section apply in sections 21.1 to 21.3.

Definition.Text: beer or malt liquor means beer or malt liquor, within the meaning of section 4 of the Excise Act, of tariff item No. 2202.90.10, heading No. 22.03 or tariff item No. 2206.00.80 or 2206.00.91, that is classified under that heading or tariff item or with the container in which it is imported.
bulk has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001.
excise warehouse has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001.
excise warehouse licensee has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001.
licensed user has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001.
packaged has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001.
specified premises has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001.
spirits means spirits, as defined in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: of an alcoholic strength by volume exceeding 22.9%, of tariff item No. 2204.10.90, 2204.21.32, 2204.21.49, 2204.29.32, 2204.29.49, 2204.30.90, 2205.10.30, 2205.90.30, 2206.00.19, 2206.00.22, 2206.00.39, 2206.00.49, 2206.00.72 or 2206.00.93, that are classified under that tariff item or with the container in which they are imported; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of heading No. 22.07 or 22.08, other than of tariff item No. 2207.20.11, 2207.20.12, 2207.20.90 or 2208.90.30, that are classified under that heading or with the container in which they are imported.

Definition.Text: wine means wine, as defined in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001, of heading No. 22.04, 22.05 or 22.06, other than of tariff item No. 2204.10.90, 2204.21.32, 2204.21.49, 2204.29.32, 2204.29.49, 2204.30.90, 2205.10.30, 2205.90.30, 2206.00.19, 2206.00.22, 2206.00.39, 2206.00.49, 2206.00.72, 2206.00.80, 2206.00.91 or 2206.00.93, that is classified under that heading or with the container in which it is imported.

Subsection.Text: In addition to any other duties imposed under this Act or any other Act of Parliament relating to customs, there is levied on bulk spirits, at the time they are imported, an additional duty equal to the duty that would be imposed on the spirits under section 122 of the Excise Act, 2001 if the spirits had been produced in Canada.

The duty levied on bulk spirits shall be paid and collected under the Excise Act, 2001, and interest and penalties shall be imposed, calculated, paid and collected under that Act, as if the duty were duty imposed on the spirits under that Act, and, for those purposes, that Act applies with any modifications that the circumstances require.

Despite subsection (2) and the Excise Act, 2001, the person who is liable for duty imposed under subsection (1) in respect of bulk spirits that have not been released under the Customs Act is the person who is liable to pay duties under the Customs Act.

In addition to any other duties imposed under this Act or any other Act of Parliament relating to customs, there is levied on packaged spirits, at the time they are imported, and paid in accordance with the Customs Act, an additional duty equal to the duty that would be imposed on them under section 122 or 123 of the Excise Act, 2001 if they had been produced and packaged in Canada.

In addition to any other duties imposed under this Act or any other Act of Parliament relating to customs, there is levied on packaged wine, at the time it is imported, and paid in accordance with the Customs Act, an additional duty equal to the duty that would be imposed on it under section 135 of the Excise Act, 2001 if it had been packaged in Canada.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Goods entered into warehouse or premises
Subsection.Text: If, immediately after being released under the Customs Act, packaged spirits or wine is entered into the excise warehouse of the excise warehouse licensee or the specified premises of the licensed user who imported the spirits or wine, the duty levied on the spirits or wine under subsection (1) or (2) shall be paid and collected under the Excise Act, 2001. Interest and penalties shall be imposed, calculated, paid and collected under the Excise Act, 2001 as if the duty were imposed under that Act, and, for those purposes, that Act applies with any modifications that the circumstances require.

Section.Text: In addition to any other duties imposed under this Act or any other Act of Parliament relating to customs, there is levied on beer or malt liquor, at the time it is imported, and paid in accordance with the Customs Act, an additional duty equal to the duty that would be levied on it under section 170 of the Excise Act if it had been manufactured or produced in Canada.
The definition duties in section 80 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: duties, other than for the purposes of section 106, means duties or taxes levied or imposed on imported goods under Part 2, the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act, the Special Import Measures Act or any other Act of Parliament relating to customs, but for the purposes of sections 89 and 113 does not include the goods and services tax.

Section.Text: Paragraph 83(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of goods that would have been classified under tariff item No. 9804.10.00 or 9804.20.00, the value for duty of the goods shall be reduced by an amount equal to that maximum specified value and, in the case of alcoholic beverages and tobacco, the quantity of those goods shall, for the purposes of assessing duties other than a duty under section 54 of the Excise Act, 2001, be reduced by the quantity of alcoholic beverages and tobacco up to the maximum quantities specified in tariff item No. 9804.10.00 or 9804.20.00, as the case may be;

Section.Text: Subsection 89(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception for tobacco products or designated goods
Subsection.Text: Relief of the duties or taxes levied or imposed under sections 21.1 to 21.3, the Excise Act, 2001 or the Excise Tax Act may not be granted under subsection (1) on tobacco products or designated goods.

Section.Text: Subsection 92(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: This section does not apply to any duty imposed under the Excise Act, 2001 in respect of manufactured tobacco that is manufactured in Canada.

Section.Text: Section 94 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In sections 95 and 96, customs duties means customs duties imposed under Part 2, other than

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: additional customs duties levied under sections 21.1 to 21.3;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: surtaxes imposed under section 53, 55, 60, 63, 68 or 78; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: temporary duties imposed under any of sections 69 to 76.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, in sections 95 and 96, customs duties does not include any duties or taxes levied or imposed on imported goods under the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act or the Special Import Measures Act.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 99(a)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the circumstances in which, and the classes of goods in respect of which, relief of duties levied under sections 21.1 to 21.3 or under the Special Import Measures Act, a surtax imposed under section 53, 55, 60, 63, 68 or 78, a temporary duty imposed under any of sections 69 to 76, a tax levied under the Excise Tax Act or a duty imposed under the Excise Act, 2001 may not be granted,

Section.Text: Subsection 106(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Temporary relief of certain duties and taxes

Subsection.Text: If an application for relief is made in the prescribed circumstances by a person of a prescribed class and in the prescribed form and manner, accompanied by prescribed documents and by security of a prescribed nature in an amount fixed by the Minister of National Revenue, relief shall be granted from the payment of the whole or the prescribed portion, as the case may be, of any duty imposed under sections 21.1 to 21.3 or the Excise Act, 2001 or of any excise taxes that, but for this section, would be payable in respect of prescribed goods that are imported and subsequently exported after being used in Canada only for a prescribed purpose.
Subsection 113(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: No refund or drawback in respect of tobacco products
Subsection.Text: No refund or drawback of the duties imposed on tobacco products under the Excise Act, 2001 shall be granted under subsection (1), except if a refund of the whole or the portion of the duties is required to be granted under Division 3.
Paragraph 113(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the circumstances in which, and the classes of goods in respect of which, a refund or drawback of duties levied under sections 21.1 to 21.3 or the Special Import Measures Act, a surtax levied under section 53, 55, 60, 63, 68 or 78, a temporary duty levied under any of sections 69 to 76, a tax levied under the Excise Tax Act or a duty levied under the Excise Act, 2001 may not be granted under subsection (1);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 113(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite the exception in subsection 89(2), a refund or drawback of duties or taxes levied or imposed under sections 21.1 to 21.3, the Excise Act, 2001 or the Excise Tax Act shall be granted under paragraph (1)(a) on designated goods.

Section.Text: Tariff item Nos. 2204.10.00, 2204.21.40, 2204.29.40, 2204.30.00, 2206.00.30, 2206.00.40, 2206.00.91, 2206.00.92 and 2208.90.91 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act are repealed.

The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 2206.00.11 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference to “Sparkling” with a reference to “Sparkling, of an alcoholic strength by volume not exceeding 22.9% vol”.
The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 2207.20.11 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Specially denatured alcohol, within the meaning of the Excise Act, 2001

Section.Text: The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 2208.90.98 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Other, packaged, of an alcoholic strength by volume not exceeding 7%359.

Section.Text: The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 2208.90.99 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Other

Section.Text: Note 4 to Chapter 98 of the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: For the purpose of this Chapter, duties means duties or taxes levied or imposed on imported goods under Part 2 of this Act, the Excise Act, 2001 (other than section 54), the Excise Tax Act, the Special Import Measures Act or any other Act of Parliament relating to customs.

Section.Text: The Description of Goods of heading No. 98.26 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference to “sections 21 and 22 of this Act” with a reference to “sections 21.1 to 22 of this Act”.
The List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in numerical order, the tariff provisions set out in Schedule 7 to this Act.
The Excise Act is amended by adding the following after section 1:

Subsection.Text: Despite anything in this Act, on the coming into force of Parts 3 and 4 of the Excise Act, 2001, this Act ceases to apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the manufacture of any goods or substance other than beer, malt liquor and any product manufactured in accordance with subsection 169(2); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the handling of, or the dealing with, anything that is or relates to any goods or substance other than beer, malt liquor and any product manufactured in accordance with subsection 169(2), to the extent that the Excise Act, 2001 applies to that handling or dealing.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Meaning of beer and malt liquor
Subsection.Text: In subsection (1), beer and malt liquor have the meaning assigned by section 4.

Section.Text: The definition beer or malt liquor in section 4 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: beer or malt liquor means all fermented liquor brewed in whole or in part from malt, grain or any saccharine matter without any process of distillation, but does not include wine as defined in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;

Section.Text: Section 176 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to a person who is licensed as a spirits licensee under section 14 of the Excise Act, 2001 and who produces beer solely for the purpose of distilling the beer.

Section.Text: The definitions accredited representative, cigar, cigarette, manufactured tobacco and tobacco stick in subsection 2(1) of the Excise Tax Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 23(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Tax on various articles at schedule rates

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (6) to (8), whenever goods mentioned in Schedule I are imported or are manufactured or produced in Canada and delivered to a purchaser of those goods, there shall be imposed, levied and collected, in addition to any other duty or tax that may be payable under this or any other law, an excise tax in respect of the goods at the applicable rate set out in the applicable section of that Schedule, computed, if that rate is specified as a percentage, on the duty paid value or the sale price, as the case may be.
Subsection 23(3.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Part, a person who, under a contract for labour, manufactures or produces goods mentioned in Schedule I from any article or material supplied by another person, other than a manufacturer licensed for the purposes of this Part, for delivery to that other person is deemed to have sold the goods, at a sale price equal to the charge made under the contract in respect of the goods, at the time they are delivered to that other person.
Subsection 23(5) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 23(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The tax imposed under subsection (1) is not payable in the case of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: goods that are purchased or imported by a manufacturer licensed for the purposes of this Part and that are to be incorporated into and form a constituent or component part of an article or product that is subject to excise tax under this Act, if the tax on the article or product has not yet been levied under this section; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the sale of a new motor vehicle designed for highway use, or a chassis for such a vehicle, to a person described in paragraph (h) of the definition manufacturer or producer in subsection 2(1) who is a manufacturer licensed for the purposes of this Part.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 23(8) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The tax imposed under subsection (1) is not payable in the case of
Subsections 23(8.1) to (8.3) of the Act are repealed.
Subsections 23(9.2) and (9.3) of the Act are repealed.
The portion of subsection 23(10) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
If goods of any class mentioned in Schedule I that were manufactured or produced in Canada are appropriated by the manufacturer or producer for their own use, for the purposes of this Part,

Section.Text: Sections 23.1 to 23.3 of the Act are repealed.
Section 24 of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Part, the Minister may require every manufacturer or producer to give security that they will render true returns of their sales as required by section 78 or by any regulations made under it and pay any tax imposed by this Act on the sales. The security shall be in an amount of not more than two hundred and fifty thousand dollars and not less than one thousand dollars and shall be by bond of a guarantee company authorized to do business in Canada, acceptable to the Government of Canada, or by deposit of Government of Canada bonds.
Parts IV and V of the Act are repealed.
Subparagraph 48(4)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of Part III, goods described in paragraph 23(7)(a); and

Section.Text: Subsection 50(9) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 56(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On the cancellation under subsection (1) of the licence granted to any licensed wholesaler, or if the licence is cancelled at the request of the licensee, or if any such licence expires and is not renewed by the licensee, all taxes imposed by this Act are forthwith payable on all goods then in the possession of the licensee that have been purchased free of tax by virtue of the licence, which taxes shall be paid at the rate in force when the licence is cancelled or expires and is not renewed and shall be computed in accordance with paragraph 50(1)(c) and Part III.

Section.Text: Subsection 64(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who is required under Part III to pay taxes shall, from time to time as required under the regulations, apply for a licence in respect of that Part.

Section.Text: Sections 66 and 66.1 of the Act are replaced by the following:
The tax imposed under this Act is not payable if evidence satisfactory to the Minister is produced to establish

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the goods in respect of which it is imposed have been exported from Canada by the manufacturer, producer or licensed wholesaler by whom the tax would otherwise be payable in accordance with any regulations made under this Act that are applicable to the goods; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the goods in respect of which it is imposed have been sold by the operator of a duty free shop and have been exported from Canada by the purchaser of the goods, in accordance with the regulations made under the Customs Act.

Section.Text: The portion of section 67 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Taxes on goods imported by Crown
Section.Text: The tax imposed under Part III is applicable
Subsection 68.1(1) of the Act is renumbered as section 68.1 and subsection 68.1(2) of the Act is repealed.
Sections 68.17 to 68.172 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Payment where use as ships’ stores
Section.Text: If tax under Part III has been paid in respect of any goods and a manufacturer, producer, wholesaler, jobber or other dealer has sold the goods for use as ships’ stores, an amount equal to the amount of that tax shall, subject to this Part, be paid to that dealer if that dealer applies for it within two years after that sale of the goods.
Subsections 68.18(1) to (3.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If tax under Part III has been paid in respect of any goods and a person holds the goods in an unused condition in inventory on the day a licence is granted to that person under section 54 or 64 and could thereafter have obtained the goods exempt from tax under subsection 23(7), an amount equal to the amount of that tax shall, subject to this Part, be paid to that person if that person applies for it within two years after the licence was granted.

If tax under Part III has been paid in respect of any goods and a person holds the goods in an unused condition in inventory on the day a licence is granted to that person under section 55 and could thereafter have obtained the goods exempt from tax under subsection 23(6), (7) or (8), an amount equal to the lesser of the amount of that tax and the amount of tax under Part III that would be payable if the goods were acquired in a taxable transaction by that person on that day shall, subject to this Part, be paid to that person if that person applies for it within two years after the licence was granted.

No amount equal to the amount of tax under Part III shall be paid under subsection (2) to a person in respect of any goods that are not subject to tax under that Part on the day a licence is granted to that person under section 55.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 68.19(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If tax under Part III has been paid in respect of any goods and Her Majesty in right of a province has purchased or imported the goods for any purpose other than
The portion of subsection 70(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
On application, the Minister may, under regulations of the Governor in Council, grant a drawback of the tax imposed under Part III and paid on or in respect of
Subsection 70(2.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
On application, the Minister may, under section 100 of the Customs Tariff, grant a drawback of the tax imposed under Part III and paid on or in respect of goods imported into Canada.
Subsection 70(5) of the Act is repealed.
Subsections 78(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Every person who is required to pay tax under Part III shall make each month a return in the prescribed form containing prescribed information of all amounts that became payable by the person on account of that tax in the preceding month.

Every person who holds a licence granted under or in respect of Part III and whose tax payable under that Part in the preceding month is nil shall make a return as required under subsection (1) reporting that fact.
Paragraphs 78(3)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorize any person to make a return in respect of any period longer than one month but not longer than six months, if the tax payable by that person under Part III for the last preceding calendar year did not exceed four thousand eight hundred dollars; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: authorize any person whose activities that give rise to tax payable by the person under Part III are predominantly limited to a seasonal period of operation to make a return in respect of any period longer than one month but not longer than six months, if the tax payable by that person under that Part for the equivalent period in the last preceding calendar year did not exceed an average of four hundred dollars per month throughout that equivalent period.

Section.Text: Subsection 79(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No penalty or interest is payable under subsection (1) if the person liable to pay the tax pays all amounts of tax payable by them under Part III and, at the time of the payment, the total penalty and interest payable in respect of those amounts of tax is less than ten dollars.
Clause 79.1(1)(a)(i)(A) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the tax payable under Part III, other than tax payable in accordance with the Customs Act, by that person in that month, and

Subsection.Text: Clause 79.1(1)(a)(ii)(A) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the tax payable under Part III, other than tax payable in accordance with the Customs Act, by that person in that accounting period, and

Subsection.Text: Clause 79.1(1)(a)(iii)(A) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the tax payable under Part III, other than tax payable in accordance with the Customs Act, by that person in that period, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 79.1(1)(b)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate amount of tax payable under Part III, other than tax payable in accordance with the Customs Act, by that person in the last preceding calendar year ending at least ninety days, or ninety-one days if that time falls in a leap year, before that time exceeded twelve million dollars, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: was, at any time in the last preceding calendar year ending at least ninety days, or ninety-one days if that time falls in a leap year, before that time, a member of a group of associated corporations (within the meaning of section 256 of the Income Tax Act) and the aggregate amount of tax payable under Part III, other than tax payable in accordance with the Customs Act, by the group in that year exceeded twelve million dollars, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is not, at that time, authorized to make a return in accordance with a regulation made under paragraph 78(3)(b) or (c).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 79.1(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

No penalty or interest is payable under subsection (4) or (5) if the large taxpayer or other person liable to pay the instalment pays all amounts of tax payable by the taxpayer or other person under Part III and, at the time of the payment, the total penalty and interest payable in respect of the instalment is less than five dollars and in respect of those amounts of tax is less than ten dollars.

Section.Text: Subsection 80(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person holding a licence in respect of Part III shall submit to the Minister each year, within six months after the end of that person’s fiscal year, a report in the prescribed form containing details of that person’s sales, taxes paid under this Act and deductions under subsection 69(2) in the fiscal year and any other prescribed information.

Section.Text: Subsection 100(5) of the Act is repealed.
The definition excisable goods in subsection 123(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: excisable goods means beer or malt liquor (within the meaning assigned by section 4 of the Excise Act) and spirits, wine and tobacco products (within the meaning assigned by section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001);

Section.Text: Subparagraph 238.1(2)(c)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: all amounts required under this Act (other than this Part), the Customs Act, the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Income Tax Act, sections 21 and 33 of the Canada Pension Plan and section 82 and Part VII of the Employment Insurance Act to be remitted or paid before that time by the registrant have been remitted or paid, and

Section.Text: Paragraph 252(1)(b) of the Act is repealed.
Schedule II to the Act is repealed.
Section 3 of Part V of Schedule VI to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: A supply of an excisable good if the recipient exports the good without the payment of duty in accordance with the Excise Act or the Excise Act, 2001.

Section.Text: Section 1.1 of Schedule VII to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: For the purposes of section 1, duty does not include a special duty imposed under section 54 of the Excise Act, 2001.

Section.MarginalNote: R.S., c. 1 (2nd Supp.), s. 213(3) (Sch. III, s. 5)
Section.Text: Paragraph 6(1)(a) of the Export Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: no intoxicating liquor held in accordance with the Customs Act, the Excise Act or the Excise Act, 2001 shall be released or removed from any warehouse or other building or place in which the liquor is stored in any case in which the liquor proposed to be removed is destined for delivery in any country into which the importation of the liquor is prohibited by law;

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: beer has the same meaning as in section 4 of the Excise Act;
bulk has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
denature has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
excise warehouse has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
licensed distiller means a person who holds a spirits licence under section 14 of the Excise Act, 2001;
packaged has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
spirits has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
wine has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The operation of paragraph (2)(e) is suspended during the period in which paragraph (2)(c) is in force.
Paragraphs 3(2)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the carriage or transportation of intoxicating liquor into and through a province by the producer of the liquor or by a common carrier, if, during the time that the intoxicating liquor is being so carried or transported, its container is not opened or broken or any of the liquor drunk or used;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of intoxicating liquor into a province by any person who is a licensed distiller or who is duly licensed by the Government of Canada to carry on the business or trade of a brewer if the liquor

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is imported solely for the purpose of being used for blending with or flavouring the products of the business or trade of a distiller or brewer carried on by the person in the province, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is kept while in the province

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: in the case of spirits or wine, in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in the case of beer, by the person in a place or warehouse that conforms in all respects to the requirements of the law governing those places or warehouses;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of bulk spirits into a province from a NAFTA country by a licensed distiller for the purpose of being packaged by the distiller, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are entitled to the United States Tariff, the Mexico Tariff or the Mexico-United States Tariff in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are kept while in the province in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of bulk spirits into a province from Chile by a licensed distiller for the purpose of being packaged by the distiller, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are entitled to the benefit of the Chile Tariff in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are kept while in the province in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of bulk spirits into a province from the United States by a licensed distiller for the purpose of being packaged by the distiller, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are entitled to the United States Tariff in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are kept while in the province in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the transfer by a licensed distiller of any spirits produced or packaged in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 that is permitted by any Act or regulation or by special permit of the Canada Customs and Revenue Agency, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of packaged spirits, are kept in an excise warehouse of a licensed distiller and in accordance with the laws of the province in which they are kept, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of bulk spirits, are kept in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province in which they are kept.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3(3) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Subsection 9(1) of the Special Economic Measures Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Peace officers for the purposes of this Act

Subsection.Text: A person having the powers of an officer under the Customs Act, the Excise Act or the Excise Act, 2001 is deemed to be a peace officer for the purposes of this Act and sections 487 to 490, 491.l and 491.2 of the Criminal Code.

Section.Text: Subsection 2.2(2) of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, the amount in dispute in an appeal means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under the Excise Act, 2001,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of duty, refund or relief that is in issue in the appeal,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any interest under that Act that is in issue in the appeal, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount of duty, refund or relief under that Act that is likely to be affected by the appeal in any other appeal, assessment or proposed assessment of the person who has brought the appeal; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of tax, net tax and rebate, within the meaning of that Part, that is in issue in the appeal,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any interest or penalty under that Part that is in issue in the appeal, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount of tax, net tax or rebate, within the meaning of that Part, that is likely to be affected by the appeal in any other appeal, assessment or proposed assessment of the person who has brought the appeal.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 12(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Court has exclusive original jurisdiction to hear and determine references and appeals to the Court on matters arising under the Canada Pension Plan, the Cultural Property Export and Import Act, the Employment Insurance Act, the Excise Act, 2001, Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act, the Old Age Security Act and the Petroleum and Gas Revenue Tax Act, where references or appeals to the Court are provided for in those Acts.
Subsections 12(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Court has exclusive original jurisdiction to hear and determine questions referred to it under section 173 or 174 of the Income Tax Act, section 204 or 205 of the Excise Act, 2001 or section 310 or 311 of the Excise Tax Act.

The Court has exclusive original jurisdiction to hear and determine applications for extensions of time under section 166.2 or 167 of the Income Tax Act, subsection 103(1) of the Employment Insurance Act, section 197 or 199 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 304 or 305 of the Excise Tax Act, subsection 28(1) of the Canada Pension Plan or section 33.2 of the Cultural Property Export and Import Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 18.18(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of calculating a time limit for the purposes of section 18.3003 or 18.3005, the following periods shall be excluded:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the period beginning on December 21 in any year and ending on January 7 of the next year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the period during which proceedings are stayed in accordance with subsection 219(3) of the Excise Act, 2001 or subsection 327(4) of the Excise Tax Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 18.29(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The provisions referred to in subsection (1) also apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, in respect of applications for extensions of time under section 166.2 or 167 of the Income Tax Act, section 197 or 199 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 304 or 305 of the Excise Tax Act, subsection 103(1) of the Employment Insurance Act, subsection 28(1) of the Canada Pension Plan or section 33.2 of the Cultural Property Export and Import Act.

Section.Text: Section 18.3001 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Application — Excise Act, 2001 and Excise Tax Act
Section.Text: Subject to section 18.3002, this section and sections 18.3003 to 18.301 apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to an appeal under

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Excise Act, 2001 if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a person has so elected in the notice of appeal for an appeal under that Act or at any later time that may be provided in the rules of Court, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount in dispute does not exceed $25,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Part IX of the Excise Tax Act if a person has so elected in the notice of appeal for an appeal under that Act or at any later time that may be provided in the rules of Court.

Section.Text: Subsection 18.3002(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Court shall, on making an order under subsection (1), order that all reasonable and proper costs of the person who has brought the appeal be borne by Her Majesty in right of Canada where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under the Excise Act, 2001, the total of sales by the person for the prior calendar year did not exceed $1,000,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, the amount in dispute does not exceed $7,000 and the aggregate of supplies for the prior fiscal year of the person did not exceed $1,000,000.

Section.Text: Subsection 18.3007(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Court may, if the circumstances so warrant, make no order as to costs or order that the person who brought the appeal be awarded costs, notwithstanding that under the rules of Court costs would be adjudged to Her Majesty in right of Canada, or make an order that person be awarded costs, notwithstanding that under the rules of Court no order as to costs would be made, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an order has been made under subsection 18.3002(1) in respect of the appeal;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the appeal is not an appeal referred to in subsection 18.3002(3); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: under the Excise Act, 2001, the amount in dispute in the appeal does not exceed $50,000 and the aggregate of sales by the person for the prior calendar year did not exceed $6,000,000, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, the amount in dispute in the appeal does not exceed $50,000 and the aggregate of supplies for the prior fiscal year of the person did not exceed $6,000,000.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 18.3008(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under the Excise Act, 2001, the amount in dispute does not exceed $25,000 and the aggregate of sales by the person for the prior calendar year did not exceed $1,000,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, the amount in dispute does not exceed $7,000 and the aggregate of supplies for the prior fiscal year of the person did not exceed $1,000,000.

Section.Text: Subsection 18.3009(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Costs — appeal under Excise Act, 2001 or Excise Tax Act

Subsection.Text: If an appeal referred to in section 18.3001 is allowed, the Court shall reimburse to the person who brought the appeal the filing fee paid by that person under paragraph 18.15(3)(b). The Court may, in accordance with the rules of Court, award costs to that person if the judgement reduces the amount in dispute by more than one half and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under the Excise Act, 2001

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount in dispute does not exceed $25,000, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of sales by the person for the prior calendar year did not exceed $1,000,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount in dispute does not exceed $7,000, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate of supplies for the prior fiscal year of the person did not exceed $1,000,000.

Section.Text: Subsection 18.31(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If it is agreed under section 204 of the Excise Act, 2001 or section 310 of the Excise Tax Act that a question should be determined by the Court, sections 17.1, 17.2 and 17.4 to 17.8 apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, in respect of the determination of the question.

Section.Text: Subsection 18.32(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Provisions applicable to determination of a question
Subsection.Text: If an application has been made under section 205 of the Excise Act, 2001 or section 311 of the Excise Tax Act for the determination of a question, the application or determination of the question shall, subject to section 18.33, be determined in accordance with sections 17.1, 17.2 and 17.4 to 17.8, with any modifications that the circumstances require.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to amend the Customs Act and to make related amendments to other Acts

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Act entitled An Act to amend the Customs Act and to make related amendments to other Acts, being chapter 25 of the Statutes of Canada, 2001.

On the later of the coming into force of subsection 19(1) of the other Act and subsection 332(1) of this Act, the portion of subsection 28(1) of the Customs Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The operator of a sufferance warehouse, bonded warehouse or duty free shop is liable for all duties or taxes levied under the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, the Excise Act, 2001, the Excise Tax Act, the Special Import Measures Act or any other law relating to customs on goods that have been received in the warehouse or duty free shop unless the operator proves that the goods
If subsection 332(2) of this Act comes into force before subsection 19(2) of the other Act, then that subsection 19(2) is repealed on the day on which that subsection 332(2) comes into force.

If subsection 332(2) of this Act and subsection 19(2) of the other Act come into force on the same day, then that subsection 332(2) is deemed to have come into force before that subsection 19(2) and subsection (3) applies.
On the later of the coming into force of section 58 of the other Act and section 297 of this Act, the description of B in paragraph 97.29(1)(a) of the Customs Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount, if any, by which the amount assessed the transferee under subsection 297(3) of the Excise Act, 2001, subsection 325(2) of the Excise Tax Act and subsection 160(2) of the Income Tax Act in respect of the property exceeds the amount paid by the transferor in respect of the amount so assessed, and

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 100 of the other Act and section 397 of this Act, subsection 2.2(2) of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of this Act, the amount in dispute in an appeal means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part V.1 of the Customs Act, the total of all amounts assessed by the Minister of National Revenue under section 97.44 of that Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under the Excise Act, 2001,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of duty, refund or relief that is in issue in the appeal,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any interest under that Act that is in issue in the appeal, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount of duty, refund or relief under that Act, that is likely to be affected by the appeal in any other appeal, assessment or proposed assessment of the person who has brought the appeal; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of tax, net tax and rebate, within the meaning of that Part, that is in issue in the appeal,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any interest or penalty under that Part that is in issue in the appeal, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount of tax, net tax or rebate, within the meaning of that Part, that is likely to be affected by the appeal in any other appeal, assessment or proposed assessment of the person who has brought the appeal.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of subsection 101(1) of the other Act and subsection 398(1) of this Act, subsection 12(1) of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:
