The Court has exclusive original jurisdiction to hear and determine references and appeals to the Court on matters arising under the Canada Pension Plan, the Cultural Property Export and Import Act, Part V.1 of the Customs Act, the Employment Insurance Act, the Excise Act, 2001, Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, the Income Tax Act, the Old Age Security Act and the Petroleum and Gas Revenue Tax Act, where references or appeals to the Court are provided for in those Acts.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of subsection 101(2) of the other Act and subsection 398(2) of this Act, subsections 12(3) and (4) of the Tax Court of Canada Act are replaced by the following:

The Court has exclusive original jurisdiction to hear and determine questions referred to it under section 97.58 of the Customs Act, section 204 or 205 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 310 or 311 of the Excise Tax Act or section 173 or 174 of the Income Tax Act.

The Court has exclusive original jurisdiction to hear and determine applications for extensions of time under subsection 28(1) of the Canada Pension Plan, section 33.2 of the Cultural Property Export and Import Act, section 97.52 or 97.53 of the Customs Act, subsection 103(1) of the Employment Insurance Act, section 197 or 199 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 304 or 305 of the Excise Tax Act or section 166.2 or 167 of the Income Tax Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 102 of the other Act and section 399 of this Act, subsection 18.18(2) of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:
For the purpose of calculating a time limit for the purposes of section 18.3003 or 18.3005, the following periods shall be excluded:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the period beginning on December 21 in any year and ending on January 7 of the next year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the period during which proceedings are stayed in accordance with subsection 106(3) of the Customs Act, subsection 219(3) of the Excise Act, 2001 or subsection 327(4) of the Excise Tax Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 103 of the other Act and section 400 of this Act, subsection 18.29(3) of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

The provisions referred to in subsection (1) also apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, in respect of applications for extensions of time under subsection 28(1) of the Canada Pension Plan, section 33.2 of the Cultural Property Export and Import Act, section 97.51 or 97.52 of the Customs Act, subsection 103(1) of the Employment Insurance Act, section 197 or 199 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 304 or 305 of the Excise Tax Act or section 166.2 or 167 of the Income Tax Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 104 of the other Act and section 401 of this Act, section 18.3001 of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Application — Customs Act, Excise Act, 2001 and Excise Tax Act
Section.Text: Subject to section 18.3002, this section and sections 18.3003 to 18.301 apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to an appeal under

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Excise Act, 2001 if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a person has so elected in the notice of appeal for an appeal under that Act or at such later time as may be provided in the rules of Court, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount in dispute does not exceed $25,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Part V.1 of the Customs Act or Part IX of the Excise Tax Act if a person has so elected in the notice of appeal for an appeal under that Act or at such later time as may be provided in the rules of Court.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 105 of the other Act and section 402 of this Act, subsection 18.3002(3) of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:
The Court shall, on making an order under subsection (1), order that all reasonable and proper costs of the person who has brought the appeal be borne by Her Majesty in right of Canada where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part V.1 of the Customs Act, the amount in dispute does not exceed $10,000;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under the Excise Act, 2001, the total of sales by the person for the prior calendar year did not exceed $1,000,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, the amount in dispute does not exceed $7,000 and the aggregate of supplies for the prior fiscal year of the person did not exceed $1,000,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 107 of the other Act and section 403 of this Act, subparagraphs 18.3007(1)(c)(i) and (ii) of the Tax Court of Canada Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: under Part V.1 of the Customs Act, the amount in dispute does not exceed $50,000,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: under the Excise Act, 2001, the amount in dispute in the appeal does not exceed $50,000 and the aggregate of sales by the person for the prior calendar year did not exceed $6,000,000, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, the amount in dispute in the appeal does not exceed $50,000 and the aggregate of supplies for the prior fiscal year of the person did not exceed $6,000,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 108 of the other Act and section 404 of this Act, paragraphs 18.3008(a) and (b) of the Tax Court of Canada Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part V.1 of the Customs Act, the amount in dispute does not exceed $10,000;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under the Excise Act, 2001, the amount in dispute does not exceed $25,000 and the aggregate of sales by the person for the prior calendar year did not exceed $1,000,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act, the amount in dispute does not exceed $7,000 and the aggregate of supplies for the prior fiscal year of the person did not exceed $1,000,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 109 of the other Act and section 405 of this Act, subsection 18.3009(1) of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Costs — appeal under Customs Act, Excise Act, 2001 or Excise Tax Act

Subsection.Text: If an appeal referred to in section 18.3001 is allowed, the Court shall reimburse to the person who brought the appeal the filing fee paid by that person under paragraph 18.15(3)(b). The Court may, in accordance with the rules of Court, award costs to that person if the judgement reduces the amount in dispute by more than one half and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part V.1 of the Customs Act, the amount in dispute does not exceed $10,000;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under the Excise Act, 2001

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount in dispute does not exceed $25,000, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of sales by the person for the prior calendar year did not exceed $1,000,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appeal under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount in dispute does not exceed $7,000, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the aggregate of supplies for the prior fiscal year of the person did not exceed $1,000,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendment to Tax Court of Canada Act
Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 110 of the other Act and section 406 of this Act, subsection 18.31(2) of the Tax Court of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

If it is agreed under section 97.58 of the Customs Act, section 204 of the Excise Act, 2001 or section 310 of the Excise Tax Act that a question should be determined by the Court, sections 17.1, 17.2 and 17.4 to 17.8 apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, in respect of the determination of the question.

Subsections (2) to (9) apply if Bill C-24, introduced in the 1st Session of the 37th Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Criminal Code (organized crime and law enforcement) and to make consequential amendments to other Acts (referred to in this section as the “other Act”), receives royal assent.
If section 4 of the other Act comes into force before section 326 of this Act, then, on the later of the day on which that section 4 comes into force and the day on which this Act is assented to,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 326 is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph (g) of the definition offence in section 183 of the Criminal Code, as enacted by that section 4, is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following provisions of the Excise Act, 2001, namely,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: section 214 (unlawful production, sale, etc., of tobacco or alcohol),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 216 (unlawful possession of tobacco product),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 218 (unlawful possession, sale, etc., of alcohol),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 219 (falsifying or destroying records),

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: section 230 (possession of property obtained by excise offences), or

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 231 (laundering proceeds of excise offences),

Subsection.Text: If section 4 of the other Act and section 326 of this Act come into force on the same day, then that section 4 is deemed to have come into force before that section 326 and subsection (2) applies.

If section 4 of the other Act comes into force after section 326 of this Act, then, on the coming into force of that section 4, paragraph (g) of the definition offence in section 183 of the Criminal Code, as enacted by that section 4, is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following provisions of the Excise Act, 2001, namely,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: section 214 (unlawful production, sale, etc., of tobacco or alcohol),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 216 (unlawful possession of tobacco product),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 218 (unlawful possession, sale, etc., of alcohol),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 219 (falsifying or destroying records),

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: section 230 (possession of property obtained by excise offences), or

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 231 (laundering proceeds of excise offences),

Subsection.Text: If section 327 of this Act comes into force before subsection 12(2) of the other Act and Bill C-36, introduced in the 1st Session of the 37th Parliament and entitled the Anti-terrorism Act, receives royal assent, then, on the later of the coming into force of section 33 of the Anti-terrorism Act and that section 327, paragraph (b.1) of the definition enterprise crime offence in section 462.3 of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: an offence against section 214, 216, 218, 230 or 231 of the Excise Act, 2001, section 153, 159, 163.1 or 163.2 of the Customs Act, subsection 52.1(9) of the Competition Act or subsection 4(1), (2), (3) or (4), or section 6, or subsection 13(1), 14(1), 16(1) or (2), 17(1), 18(1), 19(1), 20(1), 21(1) or 22(1) or section 23 of the Security of Information Act, or

Subsection.MarginalNote: Repeal of amendment in this Act to Criminal Code
Subsection.Text: If section 327 of this Act comes into force after subsection 12(2) of the other Act, then, on the later of the day on which that subsection 12(2) comes into force and the day on which this Act is assented to, that section 327 is repealed.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Repeal of amendment in this Act to Criminal Code
Subsection.Text: If section 327 of this Act and subsection 12(2) of the other Act come into force on the same day, then that section 327 is deemed to have come into force after that subsection 12(2) and subsection (6) applies.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Repeal of amendments in this Act to Customs Act
Subsection.Text: If section 62 of the other Act comes into force before sections 342 to 344 of this Act, then, on the later of the day on which that section 62 comes into force and the day on which this Act is assented to, those sections 342 to 344 are repealed.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Repeal of amendments in this Act to Customs Act
Subsection.Text: If section 62 of the other Act and sections 342 to 344 of this Act come into force on the same day, then that section 62 of the other Act is deemed to have come into force before those sections 342 to 344 and subsection (8) applies.

Section.Text: If Bill C-30, introduced in the 1st Session of the 37th Parliament and entitled the Courts Administration Service Act(referred to in this section as the “other Act”), receives royal assent, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the later of the coming into force of section 14 of the other Act and subsection 205(6) of this Act, subsection 205(6) of this Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a question set out in an application is determined by the Tax Court, the Minister or any of the persons who have been served with a copy of the application and who are named in an order of the Court under subsection (4) may, in accordance with the provisions of this Act, the Tax Court of Canada Act or the Federal Courts Act, as they relate to appeals from or applications for judicial review of decisions of the Tax Court, appeal from the determination.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the later of the coming into force of section 14 of the other Act and subsection 276(2) of this Act, subsection 276(2) of this Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Federal Courts Act and the rules made under it that are applicable to ordinary actions apply to actions instituted under subsection (1), except as varied by special rules made in respect of those actions.

Subsections (2) to (8) apply if Bill C-32, introduced in the 1st Session of the 37th Parliament and entitled the Canada-Costa Rica Free Trade Agreement Implementation Act (referred to in this section as the “other Act”), receives royal assent.
On the later of the coming into force of section 42 of the other Act and section 351 of this Act, subsection 94(1) of the Customs Tariff is replaced by the following:
In sections 95 and 96, customs duties means customs duties imposed under Part 2, other than

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: additional customs duties levied under sections 21.1 to 21.3;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: surtaxes imposed under section 53, 55, 60, 63, 68 or 78; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: temporary duties imposed under any of sections 69 to 76.1.

Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 43 of the other Act and section 352 of this Act, subparagraph 99(a)(iii) of the Customs Tariff is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the circumstances in which, and the classes of goods in respect of which, relief of duties levied under sections 21.1 to 21.3 or under the Special Import Measures Act, a surtax imposed under section 53, 55, 60, 63, 68 or 78, a temporary duty imposed under any of sections 69 to 76.1, a tax levied under the Excise Tax Act or a duty imposed under the Excise Act, 2001 may not be granted,

Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 44 of the other Act and subsection 354(2) of this Act, paragraph 113(4)(a) of the Customs Tariff is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the circumstances in which, and the classes of goods in respect of which, a refund or drawback of duties levied under sections 21.1 to 21.3 or the Special Import Measures Act, a surtax levied under section 53, 55, 60, 63, 68 or 78, a temporary duty levied under any of sections 69 to 76.1, a tax levied under the Excise Tax Act or a duty levied under the Excise Act, 2001 may not be granted under subsection (1);

Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 46 of the other Act and section 362 of this Act, each of the tariff provisions that were, under that section 362, added to the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff are amended by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: adding in the column “Preferential Tariff / Initial Rate”, below the reference to “CIAT”, a reference to “CRT: Free”; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: adding in the column “Preferential Tariff / Final Rate”, below the reference to “CIAT”, a reference to “CRT: Free (A)”.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendments in respect of Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act
Subsection.Text: If section 395 of this Act comes into force before section 53 of the other Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the later of the day on which that section 395 comes into force and the day on which the other Act is assented to, that section 53 is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the coming into force of section 37 of the other Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 3(1.1) of the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The operation of paragraph (2)(f) is suspended during the period in which paragraph (2)(c) is in force.

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraphs 3(2)(e) and (f) of the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of bulk spirits into a province from Costa Rica by a licensed distiller for the purpose of being packaged by the distiller, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are entitled to the benefit of the Costa Rica Tariff referred to in section 49.1 of the Customs Tariff, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are kept while in the province in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of bulk spirits into a province from the United States by a licensed distiller for the purpose of being packaged by the distiller, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are entitled to the United States Tariff in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are kept while in the province in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the transfer by a licensed distiller of any spirits produced or packaged in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 that is permitted by any Act or regulation or by special permit of the Canada Customs and Revenue Agency, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of packaged spirits, are kept in an excise warehouse of a licensed distiller and in accordance with the laws of the province in which they are kept, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of bulk spirits, are kept in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province in which they are kept.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendments to Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act
Subsection.Text: If section 395 of this Act comes into force after section 53 of the other Act, then, on the coming into force of section 395 of this Act,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 3(1.1) of the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The operation of paragraph (2)(f) is suspended during the period in which paragraph (2)(c) is in force.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 3(2) of the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of bulk spirits into a province from a NAFTA country by a licensed distiller for the purpose of being packaged by the distiller, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are entitled to the United States Tariff, the Mexico Tariff or the Mexico-United States Tariff in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are kept while in the province in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of bulk spirits into a province from Chile by a licensed distiller for the purpose of being packaged by the distiller, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are entitled to the benefit of the Chile Tariff in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are kept while in the province in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of bulk spirits into a province from Costa Rica by a licensed distiller for the purpose of being packaged by the distiller, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are entitled to the benefit of the Costa Rica Tariff referred to in section 49.1 of the Customs Tariff, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are kept while in the province in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the importation of bulk spirits into a province from the United States by a licensed distiller for the purpose of being packaged by the distiller, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are entitled to the United States Tariff in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are kept while in the province in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the transfer by a licensed distiller of any spirits produced or packaged in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 that is permitted by any Act or regulation or by special permit of the Canada Customs and Revenue Agency, if the spirits

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of packaged spirits, are kept in an excise warehouse of a licensed distiller and in accordance with the laws of the province in which they are kept, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of bulk spirits, are kept in accordance with the Excise Act, 2001 and the laws of the province in which they are kept.

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 3(3) of the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act is repealed.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amendments to Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act
Subsection.Text: If section 395 of this Act and section 53 of the other Act come into force on the same day, then that section 395 is deemed to have come into force after that section 53 and subsection (7) applies.

Heading.Label: PART 9
Heading.TitleText: AMENDMENTS RELATED TO EXCISE TAX ON TOBACCO PRODUCTS

Section.Text: Paragraphs 21(2)(a) to (c) of the Customs Tariff are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $0.0575 per cigarette, in the case of cigarettes;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $0.0425 per stick, in the case of tobacco sticks; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: $0.0375 per gram, in the case of manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks.

Section.Text: The definitions black stock, black stock cigarettes and Indian in subsection 2(1) of the Excise Tax Act are repealed.
Paragraphs 23.11(2)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $0.03 per cigarette, in the case of cigarettes;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $0.02415 per stick, in the case of tobacco sticks; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: $19.15 per kilogram, in the case of manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 23.12(1)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $0.0575 per cigarette, in the case of cigarettes;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $0.0425 per stick, in the case of tobacco sticks; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: $0.0375 per gram, in the case of manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 23.13(1)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $0.0575 per cigarette, in the case of cigarettes;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $0.0425 per stick, in the case of tobacco sticks; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: $37.50 per kilogram, in the case of manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 23.13(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $0.1025 per cigarette, in the case of cigarettes;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 23.13(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: $56.65 per kilogram, in the case of manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks.

Section.Text: Sections 23.31 to 23.35 of the Act are repealed.
Sections 97.1 to 97.4 of the Act are repealed.
Sections 1 to 3 of Schedule II to the Act are replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Cigarettes: $0.17138 for each five cigarettes or fraction of five cigarettes contained in any package.
Tobacco sticks: $0.02715 per stick.
Manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks: $23.148 per kilogram.

Section.Text: For the purposes of applying the provisions of the Customs Tariff and the Excise Tax Act that provide for the payment of, or the liability to pay, interest in respect of any amount, the amount shall be determined and interest shall be computed on it as though this Part had been assented to on November 2, 2001.
Sections 412 to 420 are deemed to have come into force on November 2, 2001.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 164(1)(c) of the Customs Act, chapter 1 of the Statutes of Canada, 1986, is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: designating certain classes of goods as ships’ stores for use on board a conveyance of a prescribed class, including a class based on

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the physical attributes, functions or legal descriptions of conveyances,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: areas within which conveyances voyage,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: requirements, or limitations, related to voyages of conveyances, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any combination of the bases mentioned in subparagraphs (i) to (iii);

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: limiting the quantity of goods referred to in paragraph (c) that may be used as described in that paragraph during any prescribed period or periods;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on November 10, 1986.
Paragraph 95(1)(g) of the Customs Tariff, as enacted by subsection 55(1) of chapter 41 of the Statutes of Canada, 1995, is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: designating certain classes of goods as ships’ stores for use on board a conveyance of a prescribed class, including a class based on

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the physical attributes, functions or legal descriptions of conveyances,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: areas within which conveyances voyage,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: requirements, or limitations, related to voyages of conveyances, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any combination of the bases mentioned in subparagraphs (i) to (iii);

Paragraph.Label: (g.1)
Paragraph.Text: limiting the quantity of goods referred to in paragraph (g) that may be used as described in that paragraph during any prescribed period or periods;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 1996.
Paragraph 99(g) of the Customs Tariff is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: designating certain classes of goods as ships’ stores for use on board a conveyance of a prescribed class, including a class based on

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the physical attributes, functions or legal descriptions of conveyances,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: areas within which conveyances voyage,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: requirements, or limitations, related to voyages of conveyances, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any combination of the bases mentioned in subparagraphs (i) to (iii);

Paragraph.Label: (g.1)
Paragraph.Text: limiting the quantity of goods referred to in paragraph (g) that may be used as described in that paragraph during any prescribed period or periods;

Subsection.Text: A regulation, or any provision of a regulation, made before January 1, 2004 under paragraph 99(g) or (g.1) of the Customs Tariff, as enacted by subsection (1), may, if it so provides, be retroactive and have effect for any period before it is made that begins on or after June 1, 2002.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 1998.

Section.MarginalNote: Replacement of “approvisionnements de navire” with “provisions de bord”
Section.Text: The French version of the Excise Act is amended by replacing the words “approvisionnements de navire” with the words “provisions de bord” in the following provisions, with any grammatical modifications that the circumstances require:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 52.1(e);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 58(2)(a)(i);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: clauses 58.1(6)(a)(i)(C) and (E);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 173(3)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 202(3)(c)(iii);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: section 216;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: clauses 239.1(2)(a)(i.1)(B) and (iii)(A) and (B) and subparagraph 239.1(2)(b)(vi); and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 240(2)(f) and subparagraphs 240(3)(a.1)(ii) and (c)(i) and (ii).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 35(2.3) of the Excise Tax Act, as enacted by subsection 21(3) of chapter 9 of the Statutes of Canada, 1986, is replaced by the following:
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: designating certain classes of goods as ships’ stores for use on board a conveyance of a prescribed class, including a class based on

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the physical attributes, functions or legal descriptions of conveyances,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: areas within which conveyances voyage,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: requirements, or limitations, related to voyages of conveyances, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any combination of the bases mentioned in subparagraphs (i) to (iii); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: limiting the quantity of goods referred to in paragraph (a) that may be used as described in that paragraph during any prescribed period or periods.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on November 10, 1986.
Subsection 59(3.2) of the Excise Tax Act is replaced by the following:
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: designating certain classes of goods as ships’ stores for use on board a conveyance of a prescribed class, including a class based on

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the physical attributes, functions or legal descriptions of conveyances,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: areas within which conveyances voyage,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: requirements, or limitations, related to voyages of conveyances, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any combination of the bases mentioned in subparagraphs (i) to (iii); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: limiting the quantity of goods referred to in paragraph (a) that may be used as described in that paragraph during any prescribed period or periods.

Subsection.Text: A regulation, or any provision of a regulation, made before January 1, 2004 under paragraph 59(3.2)(a) or (b) of the Excise Tax Act, as enacted by subsection (1), may, if it so provides, be retroactive and have effect for any period before it is made that begins on or after June 1, 2002.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on December 12, 1988.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 68.4:

Subsection.Text: The definitions in this subsection apply in this section.

Definition.Text: eligible ship means a ship that is a tug, ferry or passenger ship engaged in trade on an inland voyage and that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is not proceeding outside Canada other than to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a part that lies within the United States of any lake or river a part of which is included in the inland waters of Canada, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: Lake Michigan; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not engaged in international trade.

Definition.Text: inland voyage means a voyage (other than a minor waters voyage)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the inland waters of Canada, together with those parts that lie within the United States of any lake or river included in the inland waters of Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on Lake Michigan.

Definition.Text: inland waters of Canada means all the rivers, lakes and other navigable fresh waters within Canada, and includes the St. Lawrence River as far seaward as a straight line drawn

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: from Cap des Rosiers to West Point Anticosti Island; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: from Anticosti Island to the north shore of the St. Lawrence River along the meridian of longitude sixty-three degrees west.

Definition.Text: minor waters of Canada means all inland waters of Canada (other than Lake Ontario, Lake Erie, Lake Huron including Georgian Bay, Lake Superior and the St. Lawrence River east of a line drawn from Father Point to Point Orient) and includes all bays, inlets and harbours of or on those lakes or Georgian Bay.
minor waters voyage means a voyage within the minor waters of Canada together with those parts that lie within the United States of any lake or river included in the minor waters of Canada.
rebate period means the period

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that begins on June 1, 2002 and that ends on December 31, 2002;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that begins on January 1, 2003 and that ends on December 31, 2003; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that begins on January 1, 2004 and that ends on December 31, 2004.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Rebate in respect of fuel for eligible ship
Subsection.Text: If a person purchases or intends to purchase fuel that is, or is to be, used by the person to operate or maintain an eligible ship during a rebate period, subject to this Part, the Minister shall, on application by the person, pay to the person a rebate for the period determined in accordance with subsection (3).
The amount of the rebate payable to a person under subsection (2) for a rebate period is equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the amount applied for is based on an estimate, acceptable to the Minister and made within any period specified by the Minister, of the quantity of fuel that is, or is to be, purchased after May 2002 by the person and is, or is to be, used by the person to operate or maintain an eligible ship during the rebate period, the total amount of tax under Part III that would be imposed on that fuel; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the total amount of tax under Part III imposed on fuel that is purchased by the person after May 2002 and is used by the person to operate or maintain an eligible ship during the rebate period.

Subsection.Text: A person shall not file more than one application (other than an application referred to in paragraph (8)(b)) under this section for any rebate period.

If a person is paid a rebate for a rebate period based on an estimate referred to in paragraph (3)(a), the person shall, not later than 60 days after the end of the period, file with the Minister in prescribed manner a reconciliation report in prescribed form that indicates

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the rebate paid to the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of tax under Part III imposed on the fuel purchased by the person after May 2002 and used by the person to operate or maintain an eligible ship during the rebate period.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may at any time, in writing, extend the time required by subsection (5) for filing a reconciliation report.
If the Minister has, under subsection (6), extended the time required by subsection (5) for filing a reconciliation report

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the report shall be filed within the time so extended;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount of excess rebate that is required to be paid within the time otherwise required by subsection (9) shall be paid within the time so extended; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any interest or penalty payable under this section shall be calculated on the basis that the person has until the expiry of the period so extended to file the reconciliation report.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Additional amount payable to rebate recipient
Subsection.Text: If a person files a reconciliation report for a rebate period and the amount referred to in paragraph (5)(b) exceeds the amount referred to in paragraph (5)(a) in respect of the period,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister shall pay to the person an amount equal to that excess amount; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the filing of the reconciliation report is deemed to be an application to the Minister for payment of that excess amount.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Liability for excess rebate and interest up to due date of reconciliation
Subsection.Text: If the rebate paid to a person for a rebate period is determined on the basis of an estimate referred to in paragraph (3)(a) and the amount paid exceeds the amount referred to in paragraph (5)(b) in respect of the period, the person shall pay to the Receiver General

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on or before the day on or before which the reconciliation report for the rebate period is required to be filed, an amount (in this section referred to as the “excess rebate”) equal to that excess amount; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: interest at the prescribed rate, in respect of each month or fraction of a month in the period that begins on the first day following the day on which the rebate is paid to the person and that ends on the earlier of the day the total of the excess rebate and all interest under this paragraph is paid and the day on or before which the reconciliation report is required to be filed, calculated on the total of the amount of the excess rebate that has not been paid to the Receiver General, and of the amount of interest that is outstanding, in the month or fraction of a month.

Subsection.Text: The portion of the total of the excess rebate payable by a person in respect of a rebate period, and of the interest payable by the person under paragraph (9)(b), that is outstanding at the end of the day on or before which the reconciliation report for the period is required to be filed is deemed to be an amount of tax payable under this Act that is required to be, and that has not been, paid by the person on or before that day.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Interest and penalty on deemed tax
Subsection.Text: A person who is in default in paying an amount of tax referred to in subsection (10) shall pay to the Receiver General interest at the prescribed rate, and penalty of one-half of one percent, in respect of each month or fraction of a month in the period that begins on the first day following the day on or before which the reconciliation report is required to be filed and that ends on the day the total of that tax is paid, calculated on the total of the tax, penalty and interest outstanding in that month or fraction of a month.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Time for paying interest and penalty
Subsection.Text: Any interest under paragraph (9)(b) or subsection (11) and any penalty under that subsection shall be paid not later than the last day of the month in respect of which the interest or penalty was calculated.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Interest and penalty under ten dollars
Subsection.Text: No interest under paragraph (9)(b) or subsection (11) and no penalty under that subsection is required to be paid if the person who would otherwise be liable to pay the interest or the penalty pays all taxes under this section payable by the person and, on the payment, the total interest and penalty otherwise payable by the person under those provisions is less than ten dollars.
The Minister shall not, at a particular time, pay an amount to a person under this section unless the person has

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: filed with the Minister all reconciliation reports for rebate periods ending before that time for which a rebate was paid to the person that was based on an estimate referred to in paragraph (3)(a); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paid all excess rebates in respect of rebate periods ending before that time and all interest and penalty under this section that have accrued to that time.

Subsection.Text: An application may not be made under subsection (2) after December 31, 2006.

Section.MarginalNote: Replacement of “approvisionnements de navire” with “provisions de bord”
Section.Text: The French version of the Act is amended by replacing the words “approvisionnements de navire” with the words “provisions de bord”, with any grammatical changes that the circumstances require, in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 23.11(1)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 68.17(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 70(1)(b).

Section.Text: The Ships’ Stores Regulations, made by Order in Council P.C. 1986-1856 of August 13, 1986 and registered as SOR/86-878, as amended, are deemed to have been validly made and everything done under, and all consequences flowing from, those Regulations since November 10, 1986 are deemed effective as if those Regulations were so made.

The Ships’ Stores Regulations, made by Order in Council P.C. 1995-2248 of December 28, 1995 and registered as SOR/96-40, are deemed to have been validly made and everything done under, and all consequences flowing from, those Regulations since January 1, 1996 are deemed effective as if those Regulations were so made.
The Ships Suppliers Drawback Regulations are repealed.
The provisions of this Act, other than sections 1 and 408 to 432, come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

ScheduleFormHeading.Label: SCHEDULE 1
ScheduleFormHeading.TitleText: RATES OF DUTY ON TOBACCO PRODUCTS

Provision.Text: Cigarettes:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: $0.287375 for each five cigarettes or fraction of five cigarettes contained in any package, if the cigarettes are black stock

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: for delivery by the tobacco licensee who manufactured them to a duty free shop or customs bonded warehouse,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: for delivery by the tobacco licensee who manufactured them to a person for use as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations, or

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: for export by the tobacco licensee who manufactured them for delivery to a foreign duty free shop or as foreign ships’ stores; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: $0.308755 for each five cigarettes or fraction of five cigarettes contained in any package, in any other case.
Tobacco sticks:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: $0.042483 per stick, if the tobacco sticks are black stock

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: for delivery by the tobacco licensee who manufactured them to a duty free shop or customs bonded warehouse,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: for delivery by the tobacco licensee who manufactured them to a person for use as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations, or

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: for export by the tobacco licensee who manufactured them for delivery to a foreign duty free shop or as foreign ships’ stores; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: $0.045483 per stick, in any other case.
Manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: $37.483 per kilogram, if the manufactured tobacco is black stock

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: for delivery by the tobacco licensee who manufactured it to a duty free shop or customs bonded warehouse,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: for delivery by the tobacco licensee who manufactured it to a person for use as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations, or

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: for export by the tobacco licensee who manufactured it for delivery to a foreign duty free shop or as foreign ships’ stores; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: $41.481 per kilogram, in any other case.
Cigars: $14.786 per 1,000 cigars.
Raw leaf tobacco: $1.572 per kilogram.
Cigars:
The greater of

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: $0.03947 per cigar, and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: 50%, computed on

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: the sale price, in the case of cigars manufactured in Canada, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: the duty-paid value, in the case of imported cigars.

ScheduleFormHeading.Label: SCHEDULE 3
ScheduleFormHeading.TitleText: RATES OF SPECIAL DUTIES ON CERTAIN MANUFACTURED TOBACCO

Provision.Text: Special duty on imported manufactured tobacco:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: $0.0575 per cigarette, in the case of cigarettes;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: $0.0425 per stick, in the case of tobacco sticks; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: $0.0375 per gram, in the case of manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes or tobacco sticks.
Special duty on traveller’s tobacco:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: $0.0575 per cigarette, in the case of cigarettes;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: $0.0425 per stick, in the case of tobacco sticks; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: $0.0375 per gram, in the case of manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes or tobacco sticks.
Special duty on unstamped tobacco products:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: $0.0575 per cigarette, in the case of cigarettes;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: $0.0425 per stick, in the case of tobacco sticks; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: $37.50 per kilogram, in the case of tobacco products other than cigarettes or tobacco sticks.
Special duty on stamped tobacco products:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: $0.068224 per cigarette, in the case of cigarettes;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: $0.0345 per stick, in the case of tobacco sticks; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: $33.502 per kilogram, in the case of tobacco products other than cigarettes or tobacco sticks.
Spirits: $11.066 per litre of absolute ethyl alcohol contained in the spirits.
Spirits containing not more than 7% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume: $0.2459 per litre of spirits.

ScheduleFormHeading.Label: SCHEDULE 5
ScheduleFormHeading.TitleText: RATE OF SPECIAL DUTY ON SPIRITS

Provision.Text: Special duty on spirits:
$0.12 per litre of absolute ethyl alcohol contained in the spirits.
Wine:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: in the case of wine that contains not more than 1.2% of absolute ethyl alcohol by volume, $0.0205 per litre;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: in the case of wine that contains more than 1.2% of absolute ethyl alcohol by volume but not more than 7% of absolute ethyl alcohol by volume, $0.2459 per litre; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: in the case of wine that contains more than 7% of absolute ethyl alcohol by volume, $0.5122 per litre.
